-Initial proposal:
-1. Build a React frontend as a static SPA under src/frontend/web with no bundler dependency, using React + ReactDOM from ESM/CDN and Babel standalone only for development-compatible JSX transpilation in-browser if necessary, or plain createElement if reviewers reject Babel. Prefer a minimal runtime footprint and preserve direct static serving from FastAPI.
-2. Keep web_server unchanged except for serving any additional static assets from src/frontend/web.
-3. Implement design tokens in tokens.css using CSS variables for dark/light themes, typography, spacing, motion, elevation, and status colors.
-4. Implement app shell with bottom tab bar, progressive dashboard cards, timeline detail view, reusable bottom sheet, and skeleton loaders.
-5. Keep navigation client-side inside the SPA, not server-side routing.
-6. Use optimistic microinteractions, swipe affordances on mobile list rows, pull-to-refresh where feasible without degrading desktop UX, and reduced-motion fallbacks.
-7. Structure frontend into these components: AppShell, TabBar, ScreenHeader, SearchComposer, StatusCard, MetricsRail, TimelineList, TimelineItem, BottomSheet, ActionSheetForm, SearchResultsPanel, DocumentPreviewPanel, SkeletonBlock, EmptyState.

-9. Visual direction: dark-mode-first, low-chrome, information-dense, premium/confident, with typography hierarchy doing most of the work. Use brand accents sparingly, no decorative gradients that do not carry information.
-10. Performance guardrails: lazy render lower-priority panels, skeletons instead of spinners, transform/opacity-only motion, mobile-first layout, target perceived-first-paint under 1.5s on static load.
-11. Migration path: add new React entry HTML/CSS/JS assets alongside current files, switch index.html to new shell after approval, retain old files only until parity is verified.
-12. Acceptance criteria: all four agents approve IA, visual hierarchy, motion, accessibility, and performance, with concrete component/file plan suitable for implementation in this repo.
+# Production Frontend Implementation Spec v2
+
+## 0. Repo Context & Constraints
+
+- **Current state**: Frontend served as static HTML/Alpine/Tailwind from `src/frontend/web/` via `src/backend/apps/web_server.py`
+- **Backend endpoints preserved**: `/api/search`, `/api/suggest`, `/api/autocomplete`, `/api/top-searches`, `/api/search-examples`, `/api/document/{doc_id}`
+- **Stack**: React 18 functional components/hooks, Tailwind CSS core utilities, pure CSS animations, SVG icons inlined as components, CSS custom properties
+- **Breakpoints**: 375px (mobile-first) тЖТ 768px (tablet) тЖТ 1440px (desktop)
+- **Aesthetic**: Premium/confident Nubank + Flighty. Dark-mode-first. No Material/Bootstrap/shadcn defaults, no decorative chrome, no spinners, no ease/linear timing.
+
+---
+
+## 1. Build Pipeline
+
+**Vite** with `@vitejs/plugin-react` for build-time JSX transpilation, tree-shaking, minification, code splitting, and content-hashed output.
+
+```
+src/frontend/web/
+тФЬтФАтФА package.json
+тФЬтФАтФА vite.config.js
+тФЬтФАтФА index.html          тЖР Vite entry (dev)
+тФЬтФАтФА src/
+тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА main.jsx        тЖР React root mount
+тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА app/            тЖР Shell, routing, providers, API client
+тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА features/       тЖР Feature-owned screens + sub-components
+тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА shared/         тЖР Reusable UI primitives, hooks, icons
+тФВ   тФФтФАтФА styles/         тЖР tokens.css, global.css, animations.css
+тФЬтФАтФА public/             тЖР Static assets (favicon, manifest)
+тФФтФАтФА dist/               тЖР Build output (served by web_server.py)
+```
+
+**vite.config.js**:
+- `build.outDir`: `dist`
+- `build.assetsDir`: `assets`
+- `base`: `/` (or configurable for subpath deployment)
+- Output: hashed JS chunks, hashed CSS, index.html
+
+**package.json scripts**:
+- `dev`: `vite` (local dev server with HMR, proxies `/api/*` to FastAPI)
+- `build`: `vite build` (outputs to `dist/`)
+- `preview`: `vite preview`
+
+No runtime transpilation. No CDN dependencies. All dependencies vendored into the bundle at build time.
+
+---
+
+## 2. web_server.py Changes
+
+Minimal changes to existing server:
+
+1. **Serve `dist/` as static root** instead of current static files:
+   ```python
+   app.mount("/assets", StaticFiles(directory="src/frontend/web/dist/assets"), name="assets")
+   ```
+
+2. **Catch-all route** for SPA client-side routing тАФ serves `dist/index.html` for any path NOT matching `/api/*`:
+   ```python
+   @app.get("/{path:path}")
+   async def spa_fallback(path: str):
+       if path.startswith("api/"):
+           raise HTTPException(404)
+       return FileResponse("src/frontend/web/dist/index.html")
+   ```
+
+3. **Cache headers**:
+   - `dist/assets/*` (hashed filenames): `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable`
+   - `dist/index.html`: `Cache-Control: no-cache` (always revalidated)
+
+4. **During migration**: Add `/v2` route prefix that serves the new React app while `/` continues serving the legacy Alpine app. After parity is confirmed, swap `/` to serve React and move legacy files to `src/frontend/web/_legacy/`.
+
+---
+
+## 3. Design Token System (`src/styles/tokens.css`)
+
+Theme switching via `data-theme` attribute on `<html>`. Default: dark. Reads `localStorage('theme')` first, falls back to `prefers-color-scheme`, defaults to dark.
+
+### 3.1 Color Tokens
+
+```css

+  /* Dark theme (default) */
+  --color-bg-primary: #0A0A0B;
+  --color-bg-secondary: #141416;
+  --color-bg-tertiary: #1C1C1F;
+  --color-bg-elevated: #242428;
+
+  --color-surface: #1C1C1F;
+  --color-surface-hover: #28282C;
+  --color-surface-active: #303036;
+
+  --color-text-primary: #F5F5F7;        /* contrast 15.4:1 on bg-primary */
+  --color-text-secondary: #A1A1A6;      /* contrast 7.2:1 on bg-primary */
+  --color-text-tertiary: #6E6E73;       /* contrast 4.6:1 тАФ large text only */
+  --color-text-inverse: #0A0A0B;
+
+  --color-accent: #6C5CE7;              /* primary brand тАФ Nubank-inspired purple */
+  --color-accent-hover: #7D6FF0;
+  --color-accent-muted: rgba(108, 92, 231, 0.15);
+
+  --color-success: #34C759;
+  --color-success-muted: rgba(52, 199, 89, 0.15);
+  --color-error: #FF453A;
+  --color-error-muted: rgba(255, 69, 58, 0.15);
+  --color-warning: #FFD60A;
+  --color-warning-muted: rgba(255, 214, 10, 0.15);
+
+  --color-border: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08);
+  --color-border-strong: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.16);
+

+}
+

+  --color-bg-secondary: #F5F5F7;

+  --color-bg-elevated: #FFFFFF;
+
+  --color-surface: #F5F5F7;
+  --color-surface-hover: #E8E8ED;
+  --color-surface-active: #D1D1D6;
+
+  --color-text-primary: #1D1D1F;        /* contrast 18.1:1 on bg-primary */
+  --color-text-secondary: #6E6E73;      /* contrast 4.6:1 тАФ meets AA for 16px+ */

+
+**WCAG AA compliance notes**: All text-primary on bg-primary exceeds 7:1 (AAA). text-secondary meets 4.5:1 (AA) for body text. text-tertiary is used only for disabled states or decorative labels тЙе18px, meeting 3:1 large-text requirement.
+
+### 3.2 Typography Tokens
+
+System font stack. No web font loading = zero FOUT/FOIT.

+  --font-sans: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'SF Pro Display', 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;
+  --font-mono: 'SF Mono', SFMono-Regular, ui-monospace, 'Cascadia Code', Menlo, monospace;

+  --text-xs:   0.75rem;   /* 12px */  --leading-xs:   1rem;      /* 16px */

+## 4. Motion System (`src/styles/animations.css`)
+
+**Non-negotiable rules**:
+- Only `transform` and `opacity` animated (compositor-only, no layout thrash)
+- No `ease` or `linear` timing
+- All animations gated by `prefers-reduced-motion`
+
+### 4.1 Motion Tokens
+
+```css
+:root {
+  --duration-fast: 120ms;
+  --duration-normal: 200ms;
+  --duration-slow: 350ms;
+  --duration-glacial: 500ms;
+
+  /* Deceleration тАФ element entering/settling */
+  --ease-out: cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1);
+  /* Acceleration тАФ element exiting */
+  --ease-in: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 1, 0.8);
+  /* Standard тАФ repositioning/morphing */
+  --ease-standard: cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0.2, 1);
+  /* Emphasis тАФ springy/attention */
+  --ease-spring: cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1);
+
+  --stagger-delay: 40ms;
+}
+
+@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
+  :root {
+    --duration-fast: 0ms;
+    --duration-normal: 0ms;
+    --duration-slow: 0ms;
+    --duration-glacial: 0ms;
+    --stagger-delay: 0ms;
+  }
+}
+```
+
+### 4.2 Animation Patterns
+
+| Pattern | Duration | Easing | Properties |
+|---------|----------|--------|------------|
+| Screen enter | `--duration-slow` | `--ease-out` | opacity 0тЖТ1, translateY 8pxтЖТ0 |
+| Screen exit | `--duration-normal` | `--ease-in` | opacity 1тЖТ0, translateY 0тЖТ-4px |
+| Card appear | `--duration-normal` | `--ease-out` | opacity 0тЖТ1, scale 0.97тЖТ1 |
+| List item stagger | `--duration-normal` | `--ease-out` | opacity 0тЖТ1, translateY 6pxтЖТ0, delay = index ├Ч `--stagger-delay` |
+| Bottom sheet enter | `--duration-slow` | `--ease-out` | translateY 100%тЖТ0 |
+| Bottom sheet exit | `--duration-normal` | `--ease-in` | translateY 0тЖТ100% |
+| Theme toggle | `--duration-normal` | `--ease-standard` | (CSS custom property transitions handled by browser) |
+| Focus ring | `--duration-fast` | `--ease-out` | box-shadow scale |
+| Skeleton pulse | `--duration-glacial` | `--ease-standard` | opacity 0.4тЖТ0.7 (infinite alternate) |
+
+### 4.3 CSS Classes
+
+```css
+.motion-fade-in {
+  animation: fadeIn var(--duration-slow) var(--ease-out) both;
+}
+.motion-slide-up {
+  animation: slideUp var(--duration-slow) var(--ease-out) both;
+}
+.motion-scale-in {
+  animation: scaleIn var(--duration-normal) var(--ease-out) both;
+}
+
+@keyframes fadeIn {
+  from { opacity: 0; }
+  to { opacity: 1; }
+}
+@keyframes slideUp {
+  from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(8px); }
+  to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }
+}
+@keyframes scaleIn {
+  from { opacity: 0; transform: scale(0.97); }
+  to { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); }
+}
+@keyframes skeletonPulse {
+  from { opacity: 0.4; }
+  to { opacity: 0.7; }
+}
+```
+

+            тФВ                                    тФФтФАтФА <ActionForm />
+            тФВ   </Router>
+            тФВ </main>
+            тФЬтФАтФА <TabBar />           (mobile: тЙд768px)
+            тФФтФАтФА <SideRail />         (desktop: >768px)
+          </AppShell>
+        </ErrorBoundary>
+        <Toast />
+        <BottomSheet />  тЖР portal-rendered overlay
+      </DocumentProvider>
+    </SearchProvider>
+  </ThemeProvider>
+</App>
+```
+
+### 5.3 Code-Split Boundaries
+
+React.lazy + dynamic import at the **screen level**:
+- `DashboardScreen` тАФ in main bundle (critical path)
+- `SearchScreen` тАФ lazy loaded (user navigates to search)
+- `DocumentScreen` тАФ lazy loaded
+- `ActionSheet` тАФ lazy loaded
+
+Shared UI components are in the main bundle (small, reused everywhere).
+
+---
+
+## 6. Routing
+
+### 6.1 Custom Hash Router (~60 lines)
+
+Hash-based routing (`/#/`, `/#/search?q=test`, `/#/document/abc123`) to avoid any web_server.py dependency for non-API paths. The catch-all SPA route in web_server.py serves as a safety net for direct URL access.
+
+**Route map** (`routes.js`):
+
+```javascript
+export const routes = [
+  { path: '/',                    component: 'DashboardScreen' },
+  { path: '/search',              component: 'SearchScreen'    },
+  { path: '/document/:id',        component: 'DocumentScreen'  },
+  { path: '/document/:id/action', component: 'ActionSheet'     },
+];
+```
+
+**Router.jsx** responsibilities:
+- Parse hash on `hashchange` event
+- Extract path params (`:id`) and query params (`?q=`)
+- Expose `useRoute()` hook returning `{ path, params, query }`
+- Expose `navigate(path)` function
+- Manage history stack for back-button support
+- On route change: scroll to top, announce route to screen reader via aria-live region, move focus to `<h1>` of new screen
+
+### 6.2 URL State Contract
+
+| State | Storage | Reason |
+|-------|---------|--------|
+| Current route | URL hash | Deep-linkable, shareable |
+| Search query | URL query param `?q=` | Shareable search results |
+| Selected document | URL path param `:id` | Deep-linkable |
+| Theme preference | localStorage `theme` | Persists across sessions |
+| Autocomplete suggestions | Memory (SearchContext) | Ephemeral, not shareable |
+| Bottom sheet open state | Memory (DocumentContext) | Ephemeral UI state |
+
+---
+
+## 7. State Management
+
+Three isolated React Contexts with separate providers to prevent cross-domain re-renders.
+
+### 7.1 ThemeContext
+
+```javascript
+// State shape
+{ theme: 'dark' | 'light' }
+
+// Provider logic:
+// 1. Read localStorage('theme')
+// 2. Fallback: matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: light)') ? 'light' : 'dark'
+// 3. Set data-theme attribute on document.documentElement
+// 4. On toggle: update localStorage + attribute + state
+
+// Hook: useTheme() тЖТ { theme, toggleTheme }
+```
+
+### 7.2 SearchContext (useReducer)
+
+```javascript
+// State shape
+{
+  query: string,
+  status: 'idle' | 'loading' | 'error' | 'empty' | 'populated',
+  results: Array<SearchResult>,
+  autocomplete: {
+    status: 'idle' | 'loading' | 'populated',
+    suggestions: Array<string>
+  },
+  error: string | null
+}
+
+// Actions:
+// SET_QUERY тЖТ updates query, triggers debounced autocomplete
+// SET_RESULTS тЖТ results array + status
+// SET_AUTOCOMPLETE тЖТ suggestions array
+// SET_ERROR тЖТ error message + status
+// CLEAR тЖТ reset to idle
+
+// Hooks:
+// useSearch() тЖТ { state, dispatch, submitSearch, clearSearch }
+// Autocomplete: 300ms debounce via useDebounce, AbortController cancels stale requests
+```
+
+### 7.3 DocumentContext
+
+```javascript
+// State shape
+{
+  selectedId: string | null,
+  document: DocumentData | null,
+  status: 'idle' | 'loading' | 'error' | 'populated',
+  error: string | null,
+  sheetOpen: boolean
+}
+
+// Hook: useDocument() тЖТ { state, selectDocument, clearDocument, openSheet, closeSheet }
+```
+
+### 7.4 Re-render Prevention Strategy
+
+- Each context is a separate provider тАФ typing in search does NOT re-render document views
+- `React.memo` on all list item components (`SearchResultItem`, `TimelineItem`)
+- `useMemo` for derived data (filtered/sorted lists)
+- `useCallback` for event handlers passed as props
+- Autocomplete dropdown renders independently from search results
+
+---
+
+## 8. API Client (`src/app/api.js`)
+
+Single abstraction layer for all backend communication:
+
+```javascript
+// Base configuration
+const API_BASE = '/api';
+
+// Exported functions:
+async function searchDocuments(query, signal)     // GET /api/search?q={query}
+async function getAutocomplete(query, signal)     // GET /api/autocomplete?q={query}
+async function getSuggestions(query, signal)       // GET /api/suggest?q={query}
+async function getDocument(id, signal)            // GET /api/document/{id}
+async function getTopSearches(signal)             // GET /api/top-searches
+async function getSearchExamples(signal)          // GET /api/search-examples
+```
+
+**Every function**:
+- Accepts an `AbortSignal` for cancellation (component unmount / stale request)
+- Returns `{ ok: boolean, data: T, error: string | null }` тАФ never throws
+- Handles HTTP errors, network errors, and JSON parse errors uniformly
+- Logs errors to console in development
+
+**Caching strategy**:
+- `getTopSearches()` and `getSearchExamples()`: stale-while-revalidate via a simple in-memory cache with 5-minute TTL (no service worker needed for v1)
+- All other endpoints: no caching (search results must be fresh)
+
+**Request deduplication**: If an identical request is already in-flight, return the existing promise instead of firing a duplicate.
+
+---
+
+## 9. Responsive Layout Strategy
+
+### 9.1 Breakpoint Behavior
+
+| Breakpoint | Navigation | Layout | Search |
+|------------|-----------|--------|--------|
+| 375-767px (mobile) | Bottom TabBar, 56px height, 4 tabs | Single column, 16px margins | Full-width composer at top |
+| 768-1439px (tablet) | Collapsible SideRail, 64px collapsed / 240px expanded | Content area with 24px margins | Composer in header area |
+| 1440px+ (desktop) | Persistent SideRail 240px | Content area + optional sidebar panel for MetricsRail, 32px margins | Composer with Cmd+K shortcut hint |
+
+### 9.2 Navigation Transformation
+
+- **Mobile TabBar**: Fixed bottom, 4 icons (Home, Search, Documents, Settings), active state uses accent color, safe-area-inset-bottom padding for notched devices
+- **Desktop SideRail**: Fixed left, contains same 4 navigation items as vertical list with labels, collapsible to icon-only via toggle, keyboard shortcuts Cmd+1/2/3/4
+
+### 9.3 Dashboard Layout Adaptation
+
+- **Mobile**: Vertical stack тАФ SearchComposer тЖТ MetricsRail (horizontal scroll) тЖТ TopSearches тЖТ SearchExamples
+- **Tablet**: SearchComposer top, 2-column grid below (MetricsRail left, TopSearches + Examples right)
+- **Desktop**: SearchComposer top, 3-column: main content center, MetricsRail as persistent right sidebar
+
+---
+
+## 10. Screen Specifications
+
+Each screen has exactly four states: **skeleton** (loading), **populated** (data), **empty** (no data/results), **error** (failure).
+
+### 10.1 Dashboard Screen (`/`)
+
+**Skeleton**: 3 shimmer blocks (SearchComposer shape, MetricsRail cards, TopSearches list). Pulse animation on SkeletonBlock.
+
+**Populated**:
+- SearchComposer: text input with icon, placeholder "Search documents...", focus ring on interaction
+- MetricsRail: 3-4 status cards showing counts/stats from API, horizontal scroll on mobile
+- TopSearches: ordered list from `/api/top-searches`, each item tappable тЖТ navigates to `/search?q=term`
+- SearchExamples: pill/chip list from `/api/search-examples`, tappable тЖТ fills search composer
+
+**Empty**: "No data available" with subtle illustration placeholder and retry CTA button.
+
+**Error**: ErrorState component with error message, retry button, and "Check your connection" helper text.
+
+**Data loading**: `getTopSearches()` and `getSearchExamples()` fire in parallel on mount. MetricsRail data derived from same endpoints or a future stats endpoint.
+
+### 10.2 Search Screen (`/search?q=`)
+
+**Skeleton**: SearchComposer (pre-filled with query) + 6 SkeletonBlock rows simulating result items.
+
+**Populated**:
+- SearchComposer at top with current query, active/editable
+- AutocompleteDropdown: absolute-positioned below composer, max 8 suggestions, keyboard navigable (тЖСтЖУ to select, Enter to submit, Esc to dismiss)
+- SearchResultsList: virtual list of result items, each showing title (text-lg semibold), snippet (text-sm secondary), relevance indicator
+- Each SearchResultItem tappable тЖТ navigates to `/document/:id`
+
+**Empty**: "No results for '{query}'" with suggestions: "Check spelling", "Try broader terms", and links to SearchExamples.
+
+**Error**: ErrorState with retry that re-submits current query.
+
+**Interaction details**:
+- Typing triggers autocomplete after 300ms debounce
+- Each keystroke cancels previous autocomplete fetch via AbortController
+- Submitting search (Enter or button) navigates to `/search?q=newQuery` and fires `searchDocuments()`
+- Back navigation preserves search results in SearchContext (no refetch)
+
+### 10.3 Document Detail / Timeline Screen (`/document/:id`)
+
+**Skeleton**: DocumentHeader block + 5 TimelineItem skeleton rows with stagger delay.
+
+**Populated**:
+- DocumentHeader: title (text-2xl bold), document type badge, status indicator (color-coded), action button тЖТ navigates to `/document/:id/action`
+- DocumentMeta: key-value pairs (date, source, category) in a compact grid
+- TimelineList: chronological events, each TimelineItem showing timestamp (text-xs tertiary), event title (text

objections.description: Loading React + ReactDOM + Babel from CDN and transpiling JSX in-browser is not production-grade. It adds ~1MB of Babel standalone to every page load, introduces runtime parsing overhead, blocks first paint, and creates a hard dependency on third-party CDN availability. This directly contradicts the performance guardrail of perceived-first-paint under 1.5s. A production frontend must ship pre-built assets.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a minimal build step using Vite (or esbuild directly) with output to src/frontend/web/dist. Vite with React plugin produces optimized ESM bundles in <2s build time. web_server.py serves the dist/ directory as static files. This keeps the repo self-contained, requires only `npm run build`, and eliminates runtime transpilation. If the team truly cannot accept Node in CI, use esbuild as a single binary with zero npm dependencies.

objections.description: Component list is flat with no file/directory structure, no clear ownership boundaries, and no explanation of composition hierarchy. 'AppShell' containing everything is not architecture тАФ it's a God component waiting to happen. There's no error boundary strategy, no loading state machine, and no specification of which components are routes vs. shared vs. domain-specific.
objections.suggested_fix: Define explicit directory structure: src/frontend/app/{shell,routing}, src/frontend/features/{search,timeline,document}, src/frontend/shared/{ui,hooks,tokens}. Each feature directory owns its screen component, sub-components, and data-fetching hook. Specify composition: AppShell > Router > Screen > [feature components]. Add React error boundaries at the screen level. Define a state machine for each screen: idle | loading | error | empty | populated.

objections.description: 'Top-level app store via React hooks' is dangerously vague. This will either become a single useState blob that causes full-tree re-renders on every keystroke, or an ad-hoc mess of prop drilling. For a search-centric app with autocomplete, debounced input, selected document state, and theme toggling, you need to be explicit about state boundaries.
objections.suggested_fix: Define three explicit contexts with separate providers to avoid unnecessary re-renders: (1) ThemeContext (dark/light, persisted to localStorage), (2) SearchContext (query, results, autocomplete suggestions, loading state тАФ isolated so typing doesn't re-render the dashboard), (3) DocumentContext (selected doc ID, document data, panel open state). Use useReducer for SearchContext since it has multiple related state transitions. Specify that autocomplete uses a debounced fetch (300ms) with AbortController to cancel stale requests.

objections.description: Client-side routing is specified but no router implementation is named. Without React Router or a custom solution, you'll end up with conditional rendering based on string state, which breaks browser back/forward, deep linking, and URL sharing. This is a production app, not a demo.
objections.suggested_fix: Either use a lightweight hash-based router (since web_server serves index.html for all paths anyway, or configure it to do so), or implement a minimal custom router with pushState/popState (~50 lines). Define the route map explicitly: / = Dashboard, /search?q= = Search results, /document/:id = Timeline/Detail, /document/:id/action = Action sheet. Ensure web_server.py has a catch-all route that serves index.html for any path not matching /api/*.

objections.description: Token system mentions categories (typography, spacing, motion, elevation, status colors) but defines zero actual values. Without concrete tokens, the other agents (Qwen especially) cannot evaluate visual hierarchy or accessibility compliance. You can't approve what doesn't exist.
objections.suggested_fix: Include the actual token definitions in the spec. Minimum: type scale (5-7 steps with rem values and line-heights), spacing scale (4px base, 8 steps), color palette (background, surface, text-primary/secondary/tertiary, accent, error, success for both light and dark), elevation (3 levels using box-shadow with dark-mode-appropriate values), motion durations (fast: 120ms, normal: 200ms, slow: 350ms) with cubic-bezier curves (not ease or linear per requirements тАФ use something like cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1) for deceleration).

objections.description: Migration path says 'switch index.html to new shell after approval, retain old files only until parity is verified' but doesn't specify how parity is verified, who approves, or what rollback looks like. This is a flag-day migration hiding behind vague language.
objections.suggested_fix: Concrete migration: (1) New React app builds to src/frontend/web/dist/. (2) Add a route in web_server.py: /v2 serves new index.html, / continues serving old. (3) Define parity checklist: search works, autocomplete works, document detail loads, dark/light toggle works, all endpoints return data, mobile layout verified at 375px. (4) After parity confirmed, swap / to serve new app, move old files to src/frontend/web/_legacy/. (5) Remove _legacy after one release cycle.

objections.description: Swipe affordances and pull-to-refresh in a React SPA without a library are significant implementation complexity. Pull-to-refresh in particular requires touch event handling, scroll position detection, rubber-band physics, and careful interaction with browser's native pull-to-refresh on mobile Chrome/Safari. This is easily a week of work to get right and is not core to a search application.
objections.suggested_fix: Drop pull-to-refresh from v1 scope entirely. For swipe affordances on list rows, spec the exact interaction: what happens on swipe-left vs swipe-right, what's the threshold (40% of row width), what's the snap-back animation. If the answer is 'we don't know yet,' cut it from v1 and add a manual refresh button instead.

objections.description: Performance guardrails mention 'lazy render lower-priority panels' but don't define what's lower-priority. Also missing: bundle size budget, image/SVG strategy, cache headers from web_server, and whether the API responses are cacheable.
objections.suggested_fix: Define: (1) Critical render path = app shell + search bar + top searches. Everything below the fold is lazy. (2) Bundle budget: <150KB gzipped total JS including React. (3) SVG icons inlined as React components, not fetched. (4) web_server.py sets Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000 on hashed assets, no-cache on index.html. (5) API responses: cache /api/top-searches and /api/search-examples for 5min in a service worker or stale-while-revalidate.

objections.description: Acceptance criteria restates the convergence criteria from the task prompt without adding specificity. 'All four agents approve' is a process statement, not a testable acceptance criterion.
objections.suggested_fix: Add measurable criteria: Lighthouse accessibility score >= 95, color contrast ratios meet WCAG AA (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text), all interactive elements have focus-visible styles, tab order is logical, screen reader can navigate all screens, no layout shift on theme toggle, animations respect prefers-reduced-motion, works without JS for initial content (or explicitly state it doesn't and why that's acceptable for this app).

suggestions.description: Visual direction says 'dark-mode-first' but the token system should define both themes simultaneously with dark as default. Implement via a `data-theme` attribute on <html> rather than a class toggle тАФ this makes CSS selectors cleaner: `[data-theme='light']` overrides custom properties. Store preference in localStorage and check `prefers-color-scheme` as fallback on first visit.

Add an explicit API client layer. Don't scatter fetch() calls across components. Create src/frontend/app/api.js that exports typed functions: searchDocuments(query), getAutocomplete(query), getDocument(id), getTopSearches(), getSearchExamples(). Each function handles errors consistently, returns structured results, and is the single place to add auth headers, base URL config, or request interceptors later.

The spec mentions no keyboard accessibility strategy. For a search-centric app: Cmd/Ctrl+K should focus search, Escape should dismiss bottom sheets/action sheets, arrow keys should navigate autocomplete results, Enter should select. Define these upfront тАФ retrofitting keyboard nav is 3x the work.

No mention of loading/error/empty states per screen. Each of the 3 key screens needs all four states designed: skeleton (loading), populated (happy path), empty (no results/no data), error (network failure/500). The empty states especially need distinct copy and CTAs тАФ 'No results for X, try Y' is a core search UX concern.

objections.description: In-browser JSX transpilation via Babel standalone creates catastrophic performance overhead (additional 200-500KB JS parse/compile, runtime transformation cost) and violates the <1.5s perceived-first-paint target. This is not production-grade and introduces a critical dependency on client-side compilation that will fail on low-end devices.
objections.suggested_fix: Use Vite for build-time transpilation, outputting static ESM bundles to src/frontend/web/dist. Maintain FastAPI static serving while ensuring zero client-side compilation overhead and enabling tree-shaking for the <1.5s target.

objections.description: Missing deep-linking and route persistence strategy. If a user refreshes on a Document Detail view or shares a direct link, FastAPI will 404 unless the server is configured to serve index.html on all non-API routes. Additionally, the 'top-level app store via React hooks' is insufficiently specified to guarantee search context preservation during back-navigation.
objections.suggested_fix: Explicitly specify hash-based routing (e.g., #/document/123) or configure web_server.py to serve index.html on all /* routes excluding /api/*. Define a NavigationContext with explicit history stack management to preserve search filters and scroll position when navigating between Dashboard and Detail views.

objections.description: Swipe affordances on list rows conflict with standard web scrolling and text selection behaviors. Without explicit gesture boundaries and conflict resolution (e.g., touch-action CSS, swipe handles), this creates interaction ambiguity and accessibility barriers for pointer users.
objections.suggested_fix: Restrict swipe gestures to explicit drag handles (visible on touch devices) or buttons, never full-row swipe. Implement touch-action: pan-y on list containers and provide explicit undo/redo buttons for all swipe actions to ensure WCAG 2.1 compliance.

objections.description: No specification for how the information architecture transforms between mobile (375px) and desktop (1440px). A bottom tab bar on desktop creates excessive mouse travel and wastes horizontal real estate, violating the Nubank/Flighty premium density principle.
objections.suggested_fix: Define breakpoint-specific IA: bottom tab bar (mobile) transforms to collapsible side rail navigation (tablet+) with keyboard shortcuts (Cmd+1/2/3), and the MetricsRail becomes a persistent dashboard sidebar on 1440px+ to utilize viewport width.

suggestions.description: Replace atomic switchover with incremental migration using a URL-based feature flag (e.g., /v2/* routes). This allows parallel validation of the React shell against production backend data while preserving the Alpine fallback, de-risking the deployment.

Add an explicit FocusManager component to the architecture map. SPA navigation must programmatically manage focus (moving to h1 on route change, trapping focus in BottomSheet) to satisfy WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order and 2.4.7 Focus Visible requirements.

Specify a z-index elevation system in the design tokens (e.g., --z-dropdown: 100, --z-modal: 200, --z-toast: 300) to prevent visual hierarchy conflicts between BottomSheet, SearchResultsPanel, and any future toast notifications.

objections.description: Using React/Babel via CDN and in-browser transpilation is unacceptable for a 'production-grade' frontend. It introduces significant runtime overhead, causes Flash of Unstyled Content (FOUC), blocks main thread during parsing, and violates the 1.5s perceived-first-paint goal. This approach is development-only tech.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement a build step (Vite recommended) that compiles React/TSX to optimized static assets in src/frontend/web/dist. web_server serves the pre-built index.html and hashed assets. No runtime transpilation.

objections.description: Design tokens in tokens.css are too vague. 'Typography hierarchy doing most of the work' requires explicit definition to ensure premium aesthetic. Without strict type scales and font loading strategies, the visual quality will degrade to default browser styles during load.
objections.suggested_fix: Define explicit font-family stacks (system-ui + fallbacks), strict type scale (e.g., 12/14/16/20/24/32), and font-weight tokens (400/500/600/700) in tokens.css. Include font-display: swap strategies.

objections.description: BottomSheet and ActionSheet components lack specified accessibility behavior. Visual design critic requires focus trapping, scroll locking, and aria-modal attributes to be part of the spec to ensure visual focus states are meaningful.
objections.suggested_fix: Explicitly document focus management strategy (e.g., React Focus Lock) and aria-hidden state toggling for background content when sheets are open in the component spec.

suggestions.description: Migration path should include a build script in package.json that outputs directly to src/frontend/web/dist to ensure CI/CD compatibility with the existing web_server.
Define specific color contrast ratios for the dark-mode-first approach (WCAG AA minimum) within the token system to prevent accessibility debt during visual implementation.

objections.description: Babel standalone in-browser JSX transpilation is unacceptable for production. Runtime transpilation adds 200-400ms to initial load, conflicts with the 1.5s first-paint target, and ships unnecessary tooling to users. The proposal's ambiguity ('if necessary') suggests this might be intended for production use.
objections.suggested_fix: Commit to a proper build pipeline (Vite or esbuild) with pre-compiled JSX, minification, tree-shaking, and code splitting. If 'no bundler dependency' is a hard constraint, then use React.createElement directly with a .js extensionтАФno JSX. But a real bundler is strongly preferred for production-grade performance.

objections.description: ESM-from-CDN without bundling will result in waterfall requests, no tree-shaking, no minification, no code splitting, and no cache-busting. This directly undermines the stated performance guardrails and contradicts the 1.5s perceived-first-paint target.
objections.suggested_fix: Specify either: (A) a bundler-based build outputting hashed static assets to src/frontend/web/dist, or (B) if truly unbundled, quantify the performance impact with HTTP/2 multiplexing assumptions and justify how 1.5s target will be met.

objections.description: Component architecture map is a required deliverable but only a flat list of component names is provided. No hierarchy diagram, no parent-child relationships, no slot/composition patterns, no props interfaces, no state ownership boundaries.
objections.suggested_fix: Provide a concrete component tree showing: (1) nesting hierarchy, (2) which components own what state, (3) prop drilling vs context boundaries, (4) where code-split boundaries should be, (5) file/module organization under src/frontend/web/src/.

objections.description: State management strategy is underspecified: 'top-level app store via React hooks' could mean useState in App, useReducer, Context, or a library. This decision affects re-renders, memoization strategy, and performance characteristics.
objections.suggested_fix: Explicitly define: (1) state shape, (2) update patterns (useReducer + Context vs Zustand vs URL state), (3) which data lives in URL vs memory, (4) how to prevent unnecessary re-renders (React.memo boundaries, useMemo selectors).

objections.description: Performance guardrails lack concrete metrics: no JS bundle budget, no CSS size limit, no Core Web Vitals targets beyond first-paint, no lazy-loading threshold definition, no skeleton-to-content timing expectations.
objections.suggested_fix: Define measurable budgets: JS bundle < 100KB initial, CSS < 30KB, LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1. Specify which components are lazy-loaded and via what mechanism (React.lazy, IntersectionObserver, dynamic import).

objections.description: Motion system is a required deliverable but only 'transform/opacity-only' and 'reduced-motion fallbacks' are mentioned. No actual motion tokens: durations, easings, stagger patterns, enter/exit choreography.
objections.suggested_fix: Define concrete motion tokens: --duration-fast (100-150ms), --duration-normal (200-300ms), --duration-slow (400-500ms), easing curves (prefer ease-out for enters, ease-in for exits, spring-like curves for emphasis), stagger delays for list animations.

suggestions.description: Migration path lacks concrete file structure and rollback strategy. Specify the exact directory layout: src/frontend/web/src/components/, src/frontend/web/src/hooks/, src/frontend/web/src/styles/, etc. Include a feature flag or route prefix for parallel testing.

Consider using the History API with a lightweight router (or custom) to enable deep linking and back-button support, even without server-side routing. This improves UX and shareability without backend changes.
Add ErrorBoundary component to the list for graceful degradation. Add a ToastProvider or NotificationStack component for async operation feedback. Both are essential for production-grade UX.

Define API client abstraction with request deduplication, abort controller cleanup on unmount, and retry/exponential backoff strategy for failed requests. Consider a thin wrapper around fetch with typed response shapes.

+### 3.7 Theme FOUC Prevention
+
+Inline script in `index.html <head>`, executed synchronously before any paint:
+
+```html
+<script>
+  (function(){
+    var t = localStorage.getItem('theme');
+    if (!t) t = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme:light)').matches ? 'light' : 'dark';
+    document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', t);
+    document.querySelector('meta[name="theme-color"]').content = t === 'dark' ? '#0A0A0B' : '#FFFFFF';
+  })();
+</script>
+<meta name="theme-color" content="#0A0A0B">
+```
+
+This script is <300 bytes minified. It runs before React hydration, eliminating theme flash entirely.

+### 4.5 `useStaggerDelay` Hook
+
+```javascript
+/**
+ * Returns animationDelay in ms for staggered list animations.
+ * Caps total stagger at --stagger-max to prevent long cascades.
+ * @param {number} index - Item index in the list
+ * @param {number} [total] - Total number of items (for adaptive delay)
+ * @returns {string} CSS delay value, e.g. "80ms"
+ */
+function useStaggerDelay(index, total) {
+  const DELAY = 40;  // matches --stagger-delay
+  const MAX = 400;   // matches --stagger-max
+  const effectiveDelay = total && total > 0
+    ? Math.min(DELAY, MAX / total)
+    : DELAY;
+  return `${Math.min(index * effectiveDelay, MAX)}ms`;
 }
 ```

-            тФФтФАтФА <SideRail />         (desktop: >768px)
-          </AppShell>
-        </ErrorBoundary>
-        <Toast />
-        <BottomSheet />  тЖР portal-rendered overlay
-      </DocumentProvider>
-    </SearchProvider>
-  </ThemeProvider>
-</App>
-```
-
-### 5.3 Code-Split Boundaries
-
-React.lazy + dynamic import at the **screen level**:
-- `DashboardScreen` тАФ in main bundle (critical path)
-- `SearchScreen` тАФ lazy loaded (user navigates to search)
-- `DocumentScreen` тАФ lazy loaded
-- `ActionSheet` тАФ lazy loaded
-
-Shared UI components are in the main bundle (small, reused everywhere).
-
----
-
-## 6. Routing
-
-### 6.1 Custom Hash Router (~60 lines)
-
-Hash-based routing (`/#/`, `/#/search?q=test`, `/#/document/abc123`) to avoid any web_server.py dependency for non-API paths. The catch-all SPA route in web_server.py serves as a safety net for direct URL access.
-
-**Route map** (`routes.js`):
-
-```javascript
-export const routes = [
-  { path: '/',                    component: 'DashboardScreen' },
-  { path: '/search',              component: 'SearchScreen'    },
-  { path: '/document/:id',        component: 'DocumentScreen'  },
-  { path: '/document/:id/action', component: 'ActionSheet'     },
-];
-```
-
-**Router.jsx** responsibilities:
-- Parse hash on `hashchange` event
-- Extract path params (`:id`) and query params (`?q=`)
-- Expose `useRoute()` hook returning `{ path, params, query }`
-- Expose `navigate(path)` function
-- Manage history stack for back-button support
-- On route change: scroll to top, announce route to screen reader via aria-live region, move focus to `<h1>` of new screen
-
-### 6.2 URL State Contract
-
-| State | Storage | Reason |
-|-------|---------|--------|
-| Current route | URL hash | Deep-linkable, shareable |
-| Search query | URL query param `?q=` | Shareable search results |
-| Selected document | URL path param `:id` | Deep-linkable |
-| Theme preference | localStorage `theme` | Persists across sessions |
-| Autocomplete suggestions | Memory (SearchContext) | Ephemeral, not shareable |
-| Bottom sheet open state | Memory (DocumentContext) | Ephemeral UI state |
-
----
-
-## 7. State Management
-
-Three isolated React Contexts with separate providers to prevent cross-domain re-renders.
-
-### 7.1 ThemeContext
-
-```javascript
-// State shape
-{ theme: 'dark' | 'light' }
-
-// Provider logic:
-// 1. Read localStorage('theme')
-// 2. Fallback: matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: light)') ? 'light' : 'dark'
-// 3. Set data-theme attribute on document.documentElement
-// 4. On toggle: update localStorage + attribute + state
-
-// Hook: useTheme() тЖТ { theme, toggleTheme }
-```
-
-### 7.2 SearchContext (useReducer)
-
-```javascript
-// State shape
-{
-  query: string,
-  status: 'idle' | 'loading' | 'error' | 'empty' | 'populated',
-  results: Array<SearchResult>,
-  autocomplete: {
-    status: 'idle' | 'loading' | 'populated',
-    suggestions: Array<string>
-  },
-  error: string | null
-}
-
-// Actions:
-// SET_QUERY тЖТ updates query, triggers debounced autocomplete
-// SET_RESULTS тЖТ results array + status
-// SET_AUTOCOMPLETE тЖТ suggestions array
-// SET_ERROR тЖТ error message + status
-// CLEAR тЖТ reset to idle
-
-// Hooks:
-// useSearch() тЖТ { state, dispatch, submitSearch, clearSearch }
-// Autocomplete: 300ms debounce via useDebounce, AbortController cancels stale requests
-```
-
-### 7.3 DocumentContext
-
-```javascript
-// State shape
-{
-  selectedId: string | null,
-  document: DocumentData | null,
-  status: 'idle' | 'loading' | 'error' | 'populated',
-  error: string | null,
-  sheetOpen: boolean
-}
-
-// Hook: useDocument() тЖТ { state, selectDocument, clearDocument, openSheet, closeSheet }
-```
-
-### 7.4 Re-render Prevention Strategy
-
-- Each context is a separate provider тАФ typing in search does NOT re-render document views
-- `React.memo` on all list item components (`SearchResultItem`, `TimelineItem`)
-- `useMemo` for derived data (filtered/sorted lists)
-- `useCallback` for event handlers passed as props
-- Autocomplete dropdown renders independently from search results
-
----
-
-## 8. API Client (`src/app/api.js`)
-
-Single abstraction layer for all backend communication:
-
-```javascript
-// Base configuration
-const API_BASE = '/api';
-
-// Exported functions:
-async function searchDocuments(query, signal)     // GET /api/search?q={query}
-async function getAutocomplete(query, signal)     // GET /api/autocomplete?q={query}
-async function getSuggestions(query, signal)       // GET /api/suggest?q={query}
-async function getDocument(id, signal)            // GET /api/document/{id}
-async function getTopSearches(signal)             // GET /api/top-searches
-async function getSearchExamples(signal)          // GET /api/search-examples
-```
-
-**Every function**:
-- Accepts an `AbortSignal` for cancellation (component unmount / stale request)
-- Returns `{ ok: boolean, data: T, error: string | null }` тАФ never throws
-- Handles HTTP errors, network errors, and JSON parse errors uniformly
-- Logs errors to console in development
-
-**Caching strategy**:
-- `getTopSearches()` and `getSearchExamples()`: stale-while-revalidate via a simple in-memory cache with 5-minute TTL (no service worker needed for v1)
-- All other endpoints: no caching (search results must be fresh)
-
-**Request deduplication**: If an identical request is already in-flight, return the existing promise instead of firing a duplicate.
-
----
-
-## 9. Responsive Layout Strategy
-
-### 9.1 Breakpoint Behavior
-
-| Breakpoint | Navigation | Layout | Search |
-|------------|-----------|--------|--------|
-| 375-767px (mobile) | Bottom TabBar, 56px height, 4 tabs | Single column, 16px margins | Full-width composer at top |
-| 768-1439px (tablet) | Collapsible SideRail, 64px collapsed / 240px expanded | Content area with 24px margins | Composer in header area |
-| 1440px+ (desktop) | Persistent SideRail 240px | Content area + optional sidebar panel for MetricsRail, 32px margins | Composer with Cmd+K shortcut hint |
-
-### 9.2 Navigation Transformation
-
-- **Mobile TabBar**: Fixed bottom, 4 icons (Home, Search, Documents, Settings), active state uses accent color, safe-area-inset-bottom padding for notched devices
-- **Desktop SideRail**: Fixed left, contains same 4 navigation items as vertical list with labels, collapsible to icon-only via toggle, keyboard shortcuts Cmd+1/2/3/4
-
-### 9.3 Dashboard Layout Adaptation
-
-- **Mobile**: Vertical stack тАФ SearchComposer тЖТ MetricsRail (horizontal scroll) тЖТ TopSearches тЖТ SearchExamples
-- **Tablet**: SearchComposer top, 2-column grid below (MetricsRail left, TopSearches + Examples right)
-- **Desktop**: SearchComposer top, 3-column: main content center, MetricsRail as persistent right sidebar
-
----
-
-## 10. Screen Specifications
-
-Each screen has exactly four states: **skeleton** (loading), **populated** (data), **empty** (no data/results), **error** (failure).
-
-### 10.1 Dashboard Screen (`/`)
-
-**Skeleton**: 3 shimmer blocks (SearchComposer shape, MetricsRail cards, TopSearches list). Pulse animation on SkeletonBlock.
-
-**Populated**:
-- SearchComposer: text input with icon, placeholder "Search documents...", focus ring on interaction
-- MetricsRail: 3-4 status cards showing counts/stats from API, horizontal scroll on mobile
-- TopSearches: ordered list from `/api/top-searches`, each item tappable тЖТ navigates to `/search?q=term`
-- SearchExamples: pill/chip list from `/api/search-examples`, tappable тЖТ fills search composer
-
-**Empty**: "No data available" with subtle illustration placeholder and retry CTA button.
-
-**Error**: ErrorState component with error message, retry button, and "Check your connection" helper text.
-
-**Data loading**: `getTopSearches()` and `getSearchExamples()` fire in parallel on mount. MetricsRail data derived from same endpoints or a future stats endpoint.
-
-### 10.2 Search Screen (`/search?q=`)
-
-**Skeleton**: SearchComposer (pre-filled with query) + 6 SkeletonBlock rows simulating result items.

-**Populated**:

-- Each SearchResultItem tappable тЖТ navigates to `/document/:id`
-
-**Empty**: "No results for '{query}'" with suggestions: "Check spelling", "Try broader terms", and links to SearchExamples.
-
-**Error**: ErrorState with retry that re-submits current query.
-
-**Interaction details**:
-- Typing triggers autocomplete after 300ms debounce
-- Each keystroke cancels previous autocomplete fetch via AbortController
-- Submitting search (Enter or button) navigates to `/search?q=newQuery` and fires `searchDocuments()`
-- Back navigation preserves search results in SearchContext (no refetch)
-

objections.description: The artifact is truncated mid-sentence in Section 10.3 (Document Detail / Timeline Screen). The TimelineItem specification, the entire ActionSheet/Form screen (10.4), and presumably remaining sections (accessibility compliance details, performance budgets, testing strategy, migration rollback plan) are missing. This is a convergence deliverable тАФ shipping an incomplete spec means downstream agents (ZAI, Kimi, Qwen) cannot approve component structure, accessibility compliance, or performance characteristics as required by convergence criteria.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete Section 10.3 (TimelineItem populated/empty/error states, interaction details), add full Section 10.4 for ActionSheet/ActionForm screen (all four states, form validation, submission flow, error handling, keyboard/screen-reader behavior), and add remaining sections: Section 11 (Accessibility тАФ focus management, ARIA patterns per component, keyboard navigation matrix, screen reader announcements), Section 12 (Performance budget тАФ bundle size targets, LCP/FID/CLS goals, lazy loading thresholds), Section 13 (Testing strategy тАФ what gets unit tested, integration tested, and how), Section 14 (Migration rollback plan тАФ how to revert to Alpine if React deploy fails).

objections.description: The Suspense boundary for lazy-loaded screens is never specified. React.lazy is declared in 5.3 for SearchScreen, DocumentScreen, and ActionSheet, but the composition hierarchy in 5.2 shows no <Suspense> wrapper with a fallback. Without it, the lazy imports will throw when the chunk hasn't loaded yet. The fallback cannot be a spinner (per constraints), so the skeleton state needs to be explicitly wired as the Suspense fallback per screen.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a <Suspense fallback={<ScreenSkeleton />}> wrapper around <Router> in the composition hierarchy. Define ScreenSkeleton as a generic full-screen skeleton using SkeletonBlock that matches the target screen's rough layout. Alternatively, use per-route Suspense boundaries so each screen gets its own skeleton shape as fallback.

objections.description: Building a custom hash router from scratch (~60 lines claim) that handles path params, query params, history stack, scroll restoration, aria-live announcements, and focus management is a significant liability. The claim of ~60 lines is unrealistic for production-grade behavior including edge cases (double-hash in URLs, encoded params, concurrent navigation, popstate vs hashchange). This will be the #1 source of bugs. If the constraint is 'no external routing library', the spec needs to acknowledge the complexity and specify exact edge case handling. If there's no such constraint, use a battle-tested minimal router.
objections.suggested_fix: Either (a) specify that a lightweight router like wouter (3.5KB) is acceptable and use its hash-based mode, or (b) expand the Router spec to include: URL encoding/decoding strategy, handling of malformed hashes, behavior when hash contains query params with special characters, guard against navigation during pending async operations, and exact unit test cases for the router. The ~60 lines estimate needs to be either proven with pseudocode or corrected.

objections.description: The catch-all route `/{path:path}` will conflict with existing API routes depending on FastAPI route registration order. If the catch-all is registered after API routes, it works. If registered before (or if FastAPI's path matching is ambiguous for paths like `api/search` vs `{path:path}`), API calls will get index.html instead of JSON. The spec doesn't define route registration order or verify that existing web_server.py mounts API routes first.
objections.suggested_fix: Explicitly state that the catch-all MUST be the last route registered. Better: add an explicit `path.startswith('api/')` check that returns 404 (already shown in code), AND add a `path.startswith('assets/')` passthrough. Also specify that the static mount for `/assets` must be registered before the catch-all. Include a smoke test: `curl /api/search?q=test` must return JSON, not HTML.

objections.description: Light theme `--color-text-secondary: #6E6E73` on `--color-bg-primary: #FFFFFF` has a contrast ratio of approximately 4.6:1. The spec claims this 'meets AA for 16px+' but WCAG AA for normal text (<18.66px bold / <24px regular) requires 4.5:1. At 16px regular weight, this is normal text, so 4.6:1 barely passes. However, at 14px (`--text-sm`, used extensively for snippets and metadata), this is a WCAG AA failure. The secondary text color will be used on sm text throughout search results and document meta.
objections.suggested_fix: Darken `--color-text-secondary` in light theme to at least `#636366` (contrast ~5.5:1 on white) to safely pass AA at all body text sizes. Alternatively, enforce in the spec that `--color-text-secondary` is never used below `--text-base` (16px) and introduce a `--color-text-secondary-strong` for smaller text sizes.

objections.location: artifact:section-7 (State Management - no loading cancellation on unmount)
objections.description: The SearchContext and DocumentContext specs mention AbortController for autocomplete but don't specify cleanup on component unmount for the primary data fetches (searchDocuments, getDocument). If a user navigates away from DocumentScreen while getDocument is in-flight, the response will arrive and try to update state on an unmounted context consumer. The API client never throws (returns { ok, data, error }), but the dispatch will still fire into a potentially stale reducer.
objections.suggested_fix: Specify that every custom hook (useSearch, useDocument, useDashboardData) creates an AbortController in a useEffect and aborts on cleanup. The API client already accepts signals тАФ the hooks must wire them. Add this as an explicit pattern in section 7 or 8.

objections.description: Request deduplication ('if an identical request is already in-flight, return the existing promise') combined with AbortController cancellation creates a race condition. If Request A is in-flight and Component B makes the same request (gets the same promise), then Component A unmounts and aborts via its signal тАФ the shared promise is now rejected/aborted for Component B too. The dedup and abort strategies conflict.
objections.suggested_fix: Choose one: (a) Deduplicate requests but use a separate internal AbortController that only aborts when ALL consumers have aborted тАФ reference counting. (b) Don't deduplicate; rely on the debounce in useSearch to prevent rapid duplicate requests. Option (b) is simpler and correct for this use case. Remove the deduplication claim or spec the reference-counting approach precisely.

objections.description: The spec claims '4px base, powers of 2 progression' but the scale is actually linear 4px increments (4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 32, 40, 48, 64, 80) тАФ not powers of 2. This is fine as a spacing system but the documentation is incorrect, which will confuse developers trying to extend it.
objections.suggested_fix: Change the description to '4px base unit, incremental scale' or just remove the 'powers of 2' claim.

suggestions.description: The spec omits a critical shared component: a generic `AsyncState` or `DataRenderer` component that handles the four-state pattern (skeleton/populated/empty/error) consistently. Every screen repeats this pattern тАФ extracting it into a shared primitive ensures consistent behavior and reduces per-screen boilerplate. Something like `<DataView status={status} skeleton={<Skeleton />} empty={<EmptyState />} error={<ErrorState onRetry={retry} />}>{(data) => <Content data={data} />}</DataView>`.

Add a `useStaggerDelay(index)` hook that returns the computed `animationDelay` for staggered list items, capped at a max total stagger (e.g., 400ms for 10+ items). Without a cap, a list of 50 items would have a 2-second stagger cascade, which feels broken.

The migration plan mentions a `/v2` prefix but doesn't specify how the frontend build handles this. Vite's `base` config would need to be `/v2/` during the transition period, which means all asset paths change. Specify that during migration, `vite.config.js` sets `base: '/v2/'` and the server mounts at `/v2/`. After cutover, revert to `base: '/'`.

No specification for touch targets. WCAG 2.5.8 (Target Size) requires 24├Ч24px minimum with 44├Ч44px recommended. The TabBar items, SearchResultItem tap areas, and TimelineItem tap areas should all have explicit minimum touch target sizes defined (44px recommended for mobile).

No mention of error monitoring or observability. At minimum, specify that the ErrorBoundary logs caught errors to the console with structured metadata (route, component stack) and that the API client logs failed requests with status codes. For v1 this can be console-only, but the hooks should exist for a future Sentry/equivalent integration.

objections.description: MetricsRail component is specified in the Dashboard architecture but no corresponding backend endpoint exists to populate it. The current API surface only provides search, autocomplete, and document retrievalтАФno statistical metrics or analytics data. This creates an orphaned UI component with no data contract.
objections.suggested_fix: Either define a new `/api/stats` or `/api/metrics` endpoint in the backend specification, or remove MetricsRail from the v1 deliverables and add it to a post-MVP backlog. Do not ship UI components that cannot be populated.

objections.description: Critical architectural conflict: The migration strategy proposes serving the new React app at `/v2` path prefix while the Router specification mandates hash-based routing (`/#/`). Hash routing keeps the browser path at `/` permanently, making `/v2` impossible to implement as a distinct path without serving separate HTML files. This breaks the proposed migration strategy.
objections.suggested_fix: Abandon hash routing. Use HTML5 History API (pushState) with the catch-all SPA fallback in web_server.py for the new React app. Serve legacy Alpine app at `/` and React app at `/v2` during migration by checking the path prefix in the catch-all handler, then redirect `/` to `/` (React) after parity.

objections.description: The navigation specification references 4 tabs (Home, Search, Documents, Settings) but the route map only defines 3 screens plus an action sheet. There is no `/settings` route, no SettingsScreen component, and no specifications for settings functionality. The 'Documents' tab implies a list view, but only `/document/:id` (detail view) is defined.
objections.suggested_fix: Add explicit route definitions: `/settings` with SettingsScreen specifications, and either `/documents` for a list view or remove the 'Documents' tab from navigation. Ensure navigation tabs have 1:1 parity with routable screens.

objections.description: Using a bottom sheet pattern (ActionSheet) on desktop breakpoints (1440px+) is a mobile-native anti-pattern. Desktop users expect modals, side panels, or inline forms, not bottom-anchored sheets that obscure content awkwardly on wide viewports.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement responsive behavior: <768px uses BottomSheet as specified, тЙе768px transforms into a centered modal dialog or right-side panel (similar to Nubank's desktop transaction details). Define distinct desktop interaction patterns.

objections.description: The SearchContext state machine lacks explicit handling for request race conditions. If a user types 'abc' then 'abcd' rapidly, and the 'abc' autocomplete resolves after 'abcd', the UI will display stale 'abc' suggestions despite showing 'abcd' in the input. The AbortController is mentioned in API layer but not integrated into the state machine flow.
objections.suggested_fix: Add request versioning or explicit stale request rejection in the reducer. Document that every SET_QUERY action should cancel pending autocomplete requests and increment a requestId, with the reducer ignoring responses whose requestId doesn't match current.

objections.description: Focus management specification is insufficient for accessibility. While route change focuses the h1, there's no specification for: (1) Focus trapping within BottomSheet/ActionSheet (users must not tab into background), (2) Focus restoration on sheet close (return to triggering button), (3) Skip links for keyboard users to bypass navigation.
objections.suggested_fix: Add FocusTrap component implementation details using inert attribute or aria-hidden. Specify that closing ActionSheet restores focus to the triggering element (e.g., the action button in DocumentHeader). Add SkipLink component to jump to main content.

objections.description: No empty state specified for TimelineList. If a document exists but has no timeline events (valid state for new documents), the UI will show a header and blank space without explanation.
objections.suggested_fix: Add TimelineEmptyState component: 'No activity yet' with appropriate icon and contextual help text explaining that activity will appear here when available.

suggestions.description: Add route-level code splitting boundaries to the route map definition itself, specifying which routes are lazy-loaded vs bundled. This makes the performance architecture explicit.

Define keyboard shortcut discoverability. The Cmd+K shortcut for search is mentioned but not how users learn it. Add a `?` shortcut to show a command palette or shortcuts modal, following Flighty's pattern.

Specify page transition directionality. When navigating from Dashboard to Document, the motion should suggest 'forward' (slide left), and 'back' (slide right) when returning. This reinforces the navigation mental model.

objections.description: Light theme --color-text-tertiary (#AEAEB2) on --color-bg-primary (#FFFFFF) has 2.3:1 contrast ratio. Spec admits this is for decorative/disabled only but provides no enforcement mechanism. This will inevitably be misused for body text, creating WCAG AA violations.
objections.suggested_fix: Remove text-tertiary from light theme entirely or raise to minimum 4.5:1 contrast. Add CSS linting rule or design token validation that blocks tertiary color usage on text elements smaller than 18px.

objections.description: Focus ring --color-focus-ring uses rgba(108, 92, 231, 0.6) which may not achieve 3:1 contrast against all surface backgrounds, especially --color-bg-elevated (#242428) in dark mode. WCAG 2.4.7 requires visible focus indicators.
objections.suggested_fix: Define focus ring as solid 2px border with --color-accent instead of box-shadow with opacity. Add --color-focus-ring-solid: #6C5CE7 and ensure 3:1 minimum contrast against all possible backgrounds.

objections.description: No minimum touch target size defined. Mobile navigation and interactive elements must be 44x44px minimum per WCAG 2.5.5. TabBar height is 56px but individual icon tap targets are unspecified.
objections.suggested_fix: Add --touch-target-min: 44px token and document requirement that all interactive elements meet this minimum. Add --space-11: 2.75rem (44px) to spacing scale for consistent touch target sizing.

objections.description: prefers-reduced-motion sets durations to 0ms but doesn't specify fallback behavior. Some animations (screen transitions) need instant state changes, others (focus rings) should still provide visual feedback without animation.
objections.suggested_fix: Document which animations should become instant vs. which should maintain visual feedback. Add --motion-reduced: static token that components can check to determine appropriate fallback behavior.

objections.description: Theme switching reads localStorage first, then prefers-color-scheme, then defaults to dark. No mention of flash-of-unthemed-content (FOUC) prevention during initial page load before JS executes.
objections.suggested_fix: Add inline script in index.html <head> that sets data-theme attribute before React mounts. This script should be minimal (<1KB) and execute synchronously to prevent theme flash.

objections.description: Spacing scale jumps from --space-6 (24px) to --space-8 (32px), missing 28px option. Also no documentation on when to use margin vs. padding tokens, leading to inconsistent spacing patterns.
objections.suggested_fix: Add --space-7: 1.75rem (28px) for finer granularity. Add spacing usage guidelines document specifying margin patterns (external spacing) vs. padding patterns (internal spacing) for each component type.

suggestions.description: Add --color-surface-critical token for error states that needs higher contrast than standard surface colors. Current error-muted rgba values may not provide sufficient visual weight for critical error states.
Add --duration-instant: 0ms token explicitly for reduced-motion fallbacks rather than repurposing existing duration tokens. Makes intent clearer in component code.

Specify that BottomSheet must implement focus trap with first/last element focus cycling. Current spec mentions FocusTrap component but doesn't detail the implementation requirements for modal dialogs.
Add <meta name="theme-color" content="#0A0A0B"> for dark mode browser chrome coloring on mobile. Should dynamically update when theme switches via JavaScript.

objections.description: Artifact is truncated mid-sentence: 'each TimelineItem showing timestamp (text-xs tertiary), event title (text'. Missing complete specifications for Document Screen, Action Sheet/Form screen, and any subsequent sections (accessibility compliance details, performance budgets, implementation checklist, etc.). Cannot approve incomplete spec.
objections.suggested_fix: Provide complete artifact with all sections. At minimum: complete 10.3, full 10.4 Action Sheet specification, section 11 Accessibility Compliance, section 12 Performance Budgets & Metrics, section 13 Migration Checklist.

objections.description: Path check logic is incorrect: `if path.startswith('api/')` checks without leading slash, but FastAPI's path parameter in `{path:path}` may or may not include it depending on route definition. The existing API routes are mounted at `/api/*` with leading slash. This will cause API routes to incorrectly fall through to SPA fallback or vice versa.
objections.suggested_fix: Use explicit route exclusion or check for both patterns: `if path.startswith('api/') or path.startswith('/api/')`. Better yet, mount static files and SPA fallback AFTER all API routes are registered, letting FastAPI's route matching handle precedence naturally.

objections.location: Section 3.1 Color Tokens - light theme text-tertiary
objections.description: Light theme `--color-text-tertiary: #AEAEB2` claims 2.3:1 contrast ratio and is marked 'decorative/disabled only'. However, if this token is used anywhere for readable content (even timestamps, labels), it fails WCAG AA. The spec later mentions TimelineItem uses 'text-xs tertiary' for timestamps тАФ this would be inaccessible.
objections.suggested_fix: Either: (1) Increase light theme text-tertiary to minimum 3:1 for large text (e.g., #8E8E93), or (2) Add explicit constraint that text-tertiary is ONLY for truly decorative elements, disabled states, or placeholder text тАФ never for informational content like timestamps. Create a separate `--color-text-muted` token at 4.5:1 for secondary information.

objections.description: No bundle size budgets defined. Without constraints, the production build could bloat significantly, especially with lazy loading boundaries that may still pull in large dependency trees. No mention of bundle analysis or size monitoring.
objections.suggested_fix: Add performance budgets: Initial bundle тЙд100KB gzipped, lazy chunks тЙд50KB each, total JS тЙд200KB. Add `rollup-plugin-visualizer` or similar for bundle analysis. Fail CI build if budgets exceeded.

objections.description: No request timeout configuration. Fetch requests without timeout can hang indefinitely, degrading UX and potentially causing memory leaks if component unmounts while request is pending.
objections.suggested_fix: Add default timeout (e.g., 10s for search, 30s for document fetch) using AbortController with setTimeout. Make timeouts configurable per-endpoint.

suggestions.description: Add `will-change: transform, opacity` to elements entering viewport via IntersectionObserver (lazy animations). This hints the compositor to promote the layer, reducing paint cost on first animation frame. Remove will-change after animation completes to free GPU memory.

Consider preloading lazy chunks on user interaction hints: prefetch SearchScreen when user focuses search input, prefetch DocumentScreen when hovering a search result. Use `<link rel="prefetch">` or dynamic import() on hover/focus.

Consider CSS Container Queries for component-level responsiveness (e.g., MetricsRail adapting based on its container width, not viewport). This improves reusability when components are placed in different layout contexts (sidebar vs main content).

Add explicit state shape TypeScript interfaces or JSDoc type annotations. Even without TypeScript, typed documentation prevents shape drift and improves maintainability. Current spec shows state shapes but no type contracts.

+### 3.7 Focus Ring System
+
+Double-ring technique for guaranteed contrast against any surface:
+
+```css
+/* global.css */
+*:focus-visible {
+  outline: none;
+  box-shadow:
+    0 0 0 2px var(--color-bg-primary),
+    0 0 0 4px var(--color-focus-ring-solid);
+  transition: box-shadow var(--duration-fast) var(--ease-out);

+@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {

+  }
+}
+```

-### 4.2 Reduced Motion Behavior
-
-| Animation Category | Reduced Motion Behavior |
-|--------------------|------------------------|
-| Screen transitions (enter/exit) | Instant state change (0ms), no transform |
-| List stagger | Instant, all items appear simultaneously |
-| Card scale-in | Instant, appears at final scale |
-| Bottom sheet slide | Instant, appears at final position |
-| Focus ring | **Preserved** тАФ visual feedback maintained, transition to 0ms |
-| Skeleton pulse | **Replaced** тАФ static 0.5 opacity instead of pulsing |
-| Theme toggle | Instant тАФ no cross-fade |

-### 4.5 `useStaggerDelay` Hook

-```javascript
-/**
- * Returns animationDelay in ms for staggered list animations.
- * Caps total stagger at --stagger-max to prevent long cascades.
- * @param {number} index - Item index in the list
- * @param {number} [total] - Total number of items (for adaptive delay)
- * @returns {string} CSS delay value, e.g. "80ms"
- */
-function useStaggerDelay(index, total) {
-  const DELAY = 40;  // matches --stagger-delay
-  const MAX = 400;   // matches --stagger-max
-  const effectiveDelay = total && total > 0
-    ? Math.min(DELAY, MAX / total)
-    : DELAY;
-  return `${Math.min(index * effectiveDelay, MAX)}ms`;
-}
-```
