objections.description: The `/{path:path}` catch-all route will swallow ALL non-API GET requests including health checks, metrics endpoints, favicon.ico, robots.txt, and any future backend routes added outside the `/api/` prefix. The guard only checks `api/` and `assets/` prefixes тАФ any backend route like `/health`, `/ready`, `/metrics`, or webhook endpoints will silently return index.html instead of 404 or proper handler. This is a production outage waiting to happen when someone adds a non-prefixed backend route.

objections.location: Section 5.1-5.2 тАФ Router choice unspecified / wouter mentioned once
objections.description: The spec mentions 'wouter hash router integration' exactly once in Router.jsx but never specifies: (1) whether this is hash-based routing (`/#/search`) or history-based (`/search`), (2) the SPA fallback in Section 2 assumes history-based routing (clean URLs), but there's no explicit decision, (3) wouter is never listed in package.json dependencies, (4) hash routing would make the entire Section 2 SPA catch-all unnecessary since all routes would be under `/#/`. This is an architectural decision that cascades into backend serving, link sharing, SEO (if relevant), and browser back-button behavior. It must be explicitly decided and its implications documented.
objections.suggested_fix: Decide: use history-based routing with wouter's `<Router>` (requires the SPA catch-all). Add `wouter` to package.json. Document the decision explicitly in Section 1 or a new 'Routing Strategy' section. If choosing hash routing, simplify Section 2 by removing the catch-all entirely. Recommendation: history-based routing for premium UX (clean URLs, shareable links), which means the SPA catch-all is necessary but needs the fix from the previous objection.

objections.description: The API client is described as a 'single fetch abstraction' but has zero specification of: error handling strategy (what happens on 401, 403, 500, network failure, timeout?), request cancellation via AbortController (critical for search-as-you-type to avoid stale responses overwriting fresh ones), retry policy, response type validation, or how loading/error states propagate to consuming hooks. The hooks (useSearch, useDocument, useDashboardData) are listed but their state management pattern is unspecified. Without this, every feature developer will invent their own error handling, leading to inconsistent UX on failures.

The spec states `--color-text-tertiary: #6E6E73` achieves 4.6:1 on `bg-primary (#0A0A0B)`. Actual computed contrast ratio for #6E6E73 on #0A0A0B is approximately 4.53:1 тАФ barely scraping AA for normal text (тЙе4.5:1 required). This is within rounding tolerance but dangerously close. More critically, the spec says 'NOT used below 14px' but Tailwind's `text-xs` maps to 12px and `text-sm` to 14px. There's no enforcement mechanism тАФ a developer can trivially use tertiary color at 12px and violate WCAG. Similarly, when tertiary text is used on `bg-secondary (#141416)` instead of `bg-primary`, the contrast drops below 4.5:1.
objections.suggested_fix: 1. Compute actual contrast ratios for tertiary on ALL background tokens, not just bg-primary. #6E6E73 on #141416 yields ~4.1:1 which FAILS AA. 2. Either bump tertiary to #7A7A80 (~5.0:1 on bg-secondary) or add an explicit constraint: 'tertiary text ONLY on bg-primary'. 3. Add a Tailwind plugin or lint rule that flags `text-xs` combined with tertiary color class usage. 4. Document the contrast matrix as a table: each text token ├Ч each background token = pass/fail.

objections.description: DataView is listed as 'Generic 4-state renderer' but its API, the four states, and how feature screens consume it are completely unspecified. This is the single most reused pattern in the app (every screen fetches data) and without a defined contract, each screen will implement loading/error/empty/success differently. The ScreenSkeleton variants (search, document, action, settings) are also referenced but unspecified.

SearchProvider wraps the entire app but its scope is undefined. If it holds search query, results, and autocomplete state globally, every keystroke in the search input will trigger re-renders across the entire component tree (including Dashboard, Document, etc.). If it's meant to persist search state across navigation (so users can go back to results), that's valid but must use state isolation (e.g., `useSyncExternalStore` or a ref-based store) to avoid cascade re-renders.
objections.suggested_fix: Either: (A) Scope SearchProvider to only wrap search-related routes, not the entire app. Or (B) Keep it global but specify that it uses a ref-based store pattern with selective subscriptions (like Zustand or a manual `useSyncExternalStore`) so only subscribed components re-render. Document which components subscribe to which slices.

objections.location: Section 1.1 тАФ Performance budgets vs dependencies
objections.description: The 80KB initial bundle budget is aggressive (good) but there's no dependency inventory to validate feasibility. React 18 + ReactDOM is ~42KB gzipped. Wouter adds ~1.5KB. Tailwind (with purge) adds ~8-15KB depending on utility usage. That leaves ~20KB for all application code in the initial chunk. This is achievable but tight тАФ and it means zero room for any utility library (date formatting, form validation, etc.). The spec should explicitly state: no runtime dependencies beyond React, ReactDOM, and wouter. Any utility needed must be hand-rolled or the budget must be revised.
objections.suggested_fix:
Add a 'Dependency Allowlist' section:

| Package | Size (gzip) | Justification |
|---------|-------------|---------------|
| react | ~6KB | Runtime |
| react-dom | ~36KB | Runtime |
| wouter | ~1.5KB | Routing |
| **Total vendor** | **~44KB** | |
| **App budget** | **~36KB** | |

Explicitly state: 'No additional runtime dependencies without budget review. Date formatting uses Intl.DateTimeFormat. Form validation is hand-rolled. No lodash, no date-fns, no axios.'

objections.description: Screen enter/exit animations use translateX(16px/-16px) for directional navigation. This implies the app tracks navigation direction (forward vs back) to apply the correct animation class. Neither the Router nor any hook is specified to provide navigation direction state. With wouter, there's no built-in `action` property like React Router's `useNavigationType()`. Without this, the motion system for screen transitions is unimplementable as specified.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a `useNavigationDirection` hook that wraps wouter's `useLocation` and compares the current path against a ref of the previous path (or uses a route-index-based ordering) to determine forward/back direction. Alternatively, simplify: use `motion-fade-in` for all screen transitions and drop directional animation тАФ it's subtle enough at 16px that users won't notice its absence, and it eliminates a class of bugs.

objections.location: Section 4.5 тАФ useStaggerDelay missing from hook directory
objections.description: useStaggerDelay is defined inline in Section 4.5 and also listed in `shared/hooks/useStaggerDelay.js`, but the implementation shown is a plain function, not a hook (it doesn't use any React hooks). Naming it `useStaggerDelay` violates React's rules of hooks naming convention тАФ linters will flag it and developers will assume it manages state.
objections.suggested_fix: Rename to `getStaggerDelay` or `calcStaggerDelay` since it's a pure function with no hook semantics. Or wrap it in `useMemo` if the intent is to memoize per-component: `function useStaggerDelay(index, total) { return useMemo(() => calcStaggerDelay(index, total), [index, total]); }`

objections.description: The inline script calls `document.querySelector('meta[name="theme-color"]')` but the `<meta name="theme-color">` tag is placed AFTER the script in the HTML. If the browser hasn't parsed the meta tag yet (it's below the script), the querySelector returns null and `.content = ...` throws a TypeError. This is a first-load crash in the critical path.

suggestions.description: Add a `/healthz` endpoint returning `{"status": "ok"}` before the SPA catch-all, regardless of which catch-all strategy is used. Every production deployment needs a health check endpoint that returns JSON, not HTML. This should be in the spec since the catch-all could mask its absence.

Add an explicit testing strategy section. The spec has zero mention of testing тАФ no test runner (Vitest is the natural choice with Vite), no component test strategy, no accessibility testing tools (axe-core, testing-library queries), no integration test approach for API hooks. For a production frontend, this is a gap that will compound. At minimum: Vitest + @testing-library/react + axe-core for a11y assertions.

The focus ring uses `rgba(108, 92, 231, 0.6)` which may be invisible on surfaces that are close to the accent color (e.g., an accent-colored button). Add a focus ring style that uses a double-ring technique: `box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--color-bg-primary), 0 0 0 4px var(--color-focus-ring-solid)` тАФ the inner ring in the background color creates guaranteed contrast against any surface.

The ErrorBoundary wraps the entire app but there's only one level. A thrown error in DocumentScreen will take down the entire app and show a full-screen error state. Add per-route ErrorBoundaries inside each Suspense boundary so a feature crash is isolated to that screen, and the user can navigate away via TabBar/SideRail.

No mention of how the build is triggered in CI/CD or how `dist/` artifacts reach the server. Is `dist/` committed to the repo (don't), built in CI and copied, or built on the server? This determines whether `web_server.py` can assume `dist/` exists at startup. Add a build step to CI and a startup guard in web_server.py that fails fast with a clear error if `dist/` is missing.

objections.description: Architectural contradiction: ActionSheet is defined as a lazy-loaded route ('/document/:id/action') but specified to render as a BottomSheet on mobile and Modal on desktop. BottomSheets and Modals are overlay patterns that require the underlying DocumentScreen to remain mounted and visible underneath. Implementing this as a route will unmount the DocumentScreen, breaking the visual stacking context and back-button behavior. Users expect to dismiss a sheet to return to the underlying document, not navigate 'back' to a fresh document load.
objections.suggested_fix: Change ActionSheet from a route to a query param state (e.g., ?action=compose) or hash fragment (#action) within DocumentScreen. DocumentScreen should conditionally render ActionSheet as an overlay. Alternatively, use a modal route pattern where DocumentScreen renders via <Outlet /> or similar when child route is active, preserving the document view in the background.

objections.description: No specification for navigation direction detection (forward vs. back). The motion system defines slideForward and slideBack animations keyed to navigation direction, but the router implementation provides no mechanism to distinguish between entering a screen via deep link/forward navigation vs. returning via back button. Without history state management or location.key comparison, all navigation will appear as forward animations, creating disorienting UX when using browser back/forward gestures.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement a NavigationDirectionProvider that tracks location history stack. Compare current location.key with previous to determine direction. Pass direction ('forward' | 'back') as prop to animated screen wrappers, or use AnimatePresence with custom key logic to trigger correct exit/enter animations.

objections.description: Search state persistence across navigation is undefined. When a user searches on SearchScreen, clicks a result to DocumentScreen, then clicks back, the SearchScreen will remount (it's lazy-loaded) and lose query input, filters, and scroll position. This violates the 'back button returns to previous state' expectation and forces users to re-enter search terms.
objections.suggested_fix: Move search query state from SearchScreen local state to SearchProvider (global) or persist in sessionStorage. Ensure SearchScreen restores query and scroll position on remount. Alternatively, use React Router's useBlocker or state persistence hooks, or keep SearchScreen mounted in background when navigating to child routes.

objections.description: Primary navigation structure is undefined. The spec mentions TabBar (mobile) and SideRail (desktop) but lists no navigation items. Given the routes include Dashboard, Search, Document, and Settings, there's an IA conflict: Search appears both as a function within Dashboard and as a standalone route. Without explicit tab definitions, developers may implement conflicting navigation patterns (e.g., Search tab vs Search field in Dashboard).
objections.suggested_fix: Explicitly define navigation items: Dashboard (Home), Library/History (if applicable), Settings. Search should be a persistent search bar in the Shell header, not a tab, to avoid mode confusion. Document the information hierarchy: Search is a function available globally; Dashboard is the landing state; Document is a detail view; Settings is a configuration space.

objections.description: No explicit 'Up' or 'Back to results' navigation specified in DocumentScreen. Users entering from deep link have no way to reach SearchScreen except browser back (which fails if no history exists). Users entering from SearchScreen need clear affordance to return to results list, not just browser chrome.
objections.suggested_fix: Add ScreenHeader with contextual back button. If document was accessed from search (pass state via location.state), button reads 'Back to Results' and returns to /search. If accessed via deep link/direct entry, button navigates to / (Dashboard). Implement useNavigationSource hook to determine entry point.

suggestions.description: Add explicit focus management for route transitions. When navigating to a new screen, focus should move to the screen title (h1) or first interactive element. When opening ActionSheet, focus should trap within the sheet. When closing, focus should return to the trigger element. Currently, focus behavior during SPA navigation is undefined, creating accessibility gaps for screen reader users.

Implement skeleton states for progressive loading. The DocumentScreen fetches document data, but the spec shows TimelineList without loading states. Since documents may have long timelines, implement a staggered skeleton that matches the eventual content structure (header skeleton first, timeline items skeleton below) rather than generic ScreenSkeleton to reduce layout shift and perceived load time.

Clarify the 4-state renderer states. The spec mentions 'Generic 4-state renderer' but doesn't enumerate them. Explicitly define: (1) Loading (skeleton), (2) Empty (no data), (3) Error (fetch failed), (4) Success (data display). Ensure each state has defined transitions and that Error states include retry affordances.

objections.description: WCAG contrast claims are unverified and potentially incorrect. Light theme --color-text-tertiary (#86868B) on #FFFFFF is approximately 4.0:1, not 4.5:1 as claimed. This fails AA for normal text. Dark theme --color-text-tertiary (#6E6E73) on #0A0A0B needs independent verification before shipping.
objections.suggested_fix: Run actual contrast calculations using WCAG 2.1 formula. Adjust light theme tertiary to #76767B minimum (4.54:1). Add automated contrast checking to CI pipeline using axe-core or similar.

objections.description: Touch target compliance gap. Button vertical padding defined as --space-2 (8px) with typical 14-16px text height yields ~30-32px total, failing the stated --touch-target-min of 44px. This contradicts WCAG 2.5.5/2.5.8 compliance claims.
objections.suggested_fix: Define explicit button min-height: var(--touch-target-min) in component styles. Update spacing guidelines to specify --space-3 (12px) minimum vertical padding for interactive elements.

objections.description: Motion system contradicts itself. Section 4.1 states 'will-change applied only during animation, removed after completion via animationend listener' but CSS classes in 4.4 show no will-change management. The --ease-standard cubic-bezier(0.2,0,0.2,1) is functionally equivalent to ease, violating the 'no ease' rule.
objections.suggested_fix: Add will-change: transform, opacity to animated classes with JavaScript cleanup via animationend. Replace --ease-standard with distinctly non-ease curve like cubic-bezier(0.15,0,0.15,1).

objections.description: Border-width tokens completely missing. Radii defined but no --border-width-* tokens for consistent stroke weights across components. This will cause visual inconsistency in production.
objections.suggested_fix: Add border scale: --border-width-hairline: 0.5px, --border-width-default: 1px, --border-width-strong: 1.5px, --border-width-bold: 2px.

objections.description: Reduced motion skeleton handling incomplete. States skeleton pulse should be 'Replaced тАФ static 0.5 opacity' but @keyframes skeletonPulse still defined without showing how it's conditionally disabled. The prefers-reduced-motion media query only sets durations to 0ms, which would freeze skeleton at initial state.
objections.suggested_fix: Add separate .skeleton-static class with fixed opacity: 0.5 for reduced motion users. Document conditional class application in component usage guidelines.

objections.description: DataView described as 'Generic 4-state renderer' but spec never defines what the 4 states are. This ambiguity will cause implementation inconsistency across features.
objections.suggested_fix: Explicitly document: loading, empty, error, success. Add prop interface specification for each state's render callback.

suggestions.description: Add --z-dropdown-inside-modal: 450 for dropdowns rendered within modal contexts to prevent stacking issues when modals contain dropdown menus.
Add --text-4xl: 3rem with --leading-4xl: 3.5rem for dashboard metrics and large numerical displays that need visual prominence.
FOUC prevention script should also check for 'theme' in sessionStorage as fallback for cases where localStorage is disabled in private browsing modes.
Add --color-surface-disabled token for consistent disabled state styling across all interactive surfaces instead of ad-hoc opacity values.

objections.description: Artifact is truncated mid-specification. The composition hierarchy ends abruptly at `</AppShell>` without closing tags, and no actual component implementations follow. The spec defines architecture but delivers zero executable code.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the artifact with: (1) Full AppShell component implementation, (2) All 3 required screen implementations with actual JSX/hooks code, (3) Shared UI primitives (BottomSheet, Modal, DataView, etc.), (4) vite.config.js and web_server.py actual file contents, not just snippets.

objections.description: vite.config.js is described but never implemented. Performance budget enforcement via 'CI fails on exceed' has no actual mechanismтАФno rollup warning hook code, no CI script, no bundle size assertion logic.
objections.suggested_fix: Provide complete vite.config.js with build.rollupOptions.onWarn hook for bundle size warnings, and a scripts/check-bundle-size.js for CI enforcement that parses dist/assets/*.js and exits non-zero on budget violation.

objections.description: All components are file names in a directory treeтАФzero actual implementation code. Required deliverables explicitly include 'core layout shell' and 'at least 3 key screens' but none exist as runnable code.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement: (1) AppShell.jsx with responsive TabBar/SideRail, (2) DashboardScreen.jsx with SearchComposer + TopSearches + SearchExamples, (3) DocumentScreen.jsx with TimelineList, (4) ActionSheet.jsx with BottomSheet/Modal responsive pattern. Each must include hooks, styling, and accessibility attributes.

objections.description: Critical hooks are listed but not implemented: useDebounce (required for search input), useFocusManagement (required for accessibility), useMediaQuery (required for responsive BottomSheet/Modal switch), useReducedMotion (required for motion gating). Only useStaggerDelay is shown.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement all 5 hooks with full JSDoc, TypeScript-like param annotations, and actual logic. useReducedMotion must return boolean from matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)'). useMediaQuery must accept breakpoint string and return boolean.

objections.description: StaticFiles cache headers for immutable assets are described but FastAPI StaticFiles doesn't set 'immutable' by default. The middleware approach is mentioned but not implemented.
objections.suggested_fix: Add actual middleware or custom StaticFiles subclass that sets 'Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable' for /assets/* routes. Show the complete web_server.py diff.

objections.description: Inline script references meta[name='theme-color'] that doesn't exist in the shown HTML. The script queries for it before the meta tag is declared.
objections.suggested_fix: Reorder: meta tag first, then script. Or remove the theme-color update from the inline script and handle it in ThemeProvider after React mounts. Show complete index.html <head> structure.

objections.description: API client is mentioned but not implemented. No error handling strategy, no request cancellation for autocomplete (critical for search UX), no retry logic for transient failures.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement api.js with: (1) AbortController support for cancellable requests, (2) Exponential backoff retry for 5xx errors, (3) Typed error classes (NetworkError, ApiError), (4) Request deduplication for autocomplete.

suggestions.description: Lighthouse CI assertions are mentioned but no lighthouserc.js or budget.json is provided. Add actual Lighthouse CI configuration file.
Hook hardcodes DELAY=40 and MAX=400 instead of reading from CSS custom properties. Consider using CSS variables via getComputedStyle for single source of truth, or document the duplication risk.

SVG icons are listed but not implemented. For performance, each should be a memoized React component with aria-hidden=true and currentColor for theming. Show at least SearchIcon and ChevronIcon implementations.

+**Transition rules**:
+- Navigating to a higher-index screen: current screen slides left + fades, new screen slides in from right + fades in
+- Navigating to a lower-index screen (back): current screen slides right + fades, new screen slides in from left + fades in
+- Same-index navigation (e.g., Document тЖТ Document): crossfade only, no slide

+// shared/hooks/useDocumentTitle.js
+import { useEffect } from 'react';
+
+export function useDocumentTitle(title) {
+  useEffect(() => {
+    const prev = document.title;
+    document.title = title ? `${title} тАФ App` : 'App';
+    return () => { document.title = prev; };
+  }, [title]);
+}
+```

# Production Frontend Implementation Spec v5

## 0. Repo Context & Constraints

- **Current state**: Frontend served as static HTML/Alpine/Tailwind from `src/frontend/web/` via `src/backend/apps/web_server.py`
- **Backend endpoints preserved**: `/api/search`, `/api/suggest`, `/api/autocomplete`, `/api/top-searches`, `/api/search-examples`, `/api/document/{doc_id}`
- **Stack**: React 18 functional components/hooks, Tailwind CSS core utilities, pure CSS animations, SVG icons inlined as components, CSS custom properties
- **Breakpoints**: 375px (mobile-first) тЖТ 768px (tablet) тЖТ 1440px (desktop)
- **Aesthetic**: Premium/confident Nubank + Flighty. Dark-mode-first. No Material/Bootstrap/shadcn defaults, no decorative chrome, no spinners, no ease/linear timing.
- **Node requirement**: >=18.0.0 (documented in `engines` field)
- **API contract**: All URLs in API responses are relative paths. No absolute URLs returned by any endpoint.
- **Dependency policy**: Any new runtime dependency requires a PR with bundle-size-before/after from `build:analyze` and approval from 2 reviewers.

### 1.6 Navigation Flow Specification

**Spatial model**: Stack-based navigation. Screens exist on a conceptual left-to-right axis ordered: Dashboard(0) тЖТ Search(1) тЖТ Document(2).

**Transition rules**:
- Navigating to a higher-index screen: current screen slides left + fades, new screen slides in from right + fades in
- Navigating to a lower-index screen (back): current screen slides right + fades, new screen slides in from left + fades in
- Same-index navigation (e.g., Document тЖТ Document): crossfade only, no slide
- ActionSheet: slides up from bottom (not a route transition)

**Browser back button**: Triggers identical animation to in-app back navigation тАФ wouter's history integration fires the same location change, and `useNavigationDirection` detects the index decrease.

**Deep linking**: ActionSheet is NOT URL-routable. Direct link to `/document/abc123` opens DocumentScreen with ActionSheet closed. This avoids complexity of encoding overlay state in URL and prevents broken states from stale links.

**Document title updates**: `useDocumentTitle` hook called in each screen component:
```javascript
// shared/hooks/useDocumentTitle.js
import { useEffect } from 'react';

export function useDocumentTitle(title) {
  useEffect(() => {
    const prev = document.title;
    document.title = title ? `${title} тАФ App` : 'App';
    return () => { document.title = prev; };
  }, [title]);
}
```

**Route announcements for screen readers**: `AnimatedRoute` renders a visually-hidden `<div aria-live="assertive" role="status">` that announces the new screen name on route change (e.g., "Navigated to Document Details").

**scripts/check-bundle-size.js**:

```javascript
import { readdirSync, statSync, readFileSync, existsSync } from 'node:fs';
import { join, dirname } from 'node:path';
import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
import { gzipSync } from 'node:zlib';

const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
const DIST = join(__dirname, '..', 'dist', 'assets');
const BUDGETS = {
  'vendor': { max: 45_000, label: 'Vendor chunk' },
  'index':  { max: 40_000, label: 'Initial app chunk' },
};
const TOTAL_MAX = 160_000;

if (!existsSync(DIST)) {
  console.error('FAIL: dist/assets not found. Run `npm run build` first.');
  process.exit(1);
}

let total = 0;
let failed = false;

for (const file of readdirSync(DIST)) {
  if (!file.endsWith('.js')) continue;
  const filePath = join(DIST, file);
  const raw = statSync(filePath).size;
  const contents = readFileSync(filePath);
  const gzipped = gzipSync(contents, { level: 9 }).length;
  total += gzipped;

  for (const [key, budget] of Object.entries(BUDGETS)) {
    if (file.includes(key) && gzipped > budget.max) {
      console.error(
        `FAIL: ${budget.label} (${file}) is ${gzipped}B gzipped, budget ${budget.max}B`
      );
      failed = true;
    }
  }
  console.log(`  ${file}: ${raw}B raw, ${gzipped}B gzipped`);
}

if (total > TOTAL_MAX) {
  console.error(`FAIL: Total JS ${total}B gzipped exceeds budget ${TOTAL_MAX}B`);
  failed = true;
}

process.exit(failed ? 1 : 0);
```

## 2. web_server.py Changes

Minimal, surgical changes to existing server. Shown as additions to the existing file тАФ **do not replace existing API router registration**.

### 2.1 Production Serving

```python
# --- ADD these imports at top of existing web_server.py ---
import os
from pathlib import Path
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles
from fastapi.responses import FileResponse, JSONResponse
from starlette.exceptions import HTTPException as StarletteHTTPException
from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware
from starlette.requests import Request
from starlette.responses import Response

DIST_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent / "frontend" / "web" / "dist"
DIST_INDEX = DIST_DIR / "index.html"
DIST_ASSETS = DIST_DIR / "assets"

# --- ADD: Startup guard ---
def validate_frontend_build():
    """Called at app startup. Fails fast if dist/ is missing."""
    if not DIST_INDEX.exists():
        raise RuntimeError(
            f"Frontend build not found at {DIST_DIR}. "
            "Run 'cd src/frontend/web && npm run build' first."
        )

# --- ADD: Lifespan handler (replaces any existing on_event("startup")) ---
@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app_instance):
    validate_frontend_build()
    yield

# --- MODIFY: FastAPI instantiation to include lifespan ---
# Change: app = FastAPI(...)
# To:     app = FastAPI(..., lifespan=lifespan)

# --- ADD: Immutable asset cache middleware ---
class ImmutableAssetsCacheMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):
    async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next):
        response: Response = await call_next(request)
        if request.url.path.startswith("/assets/"):
            response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "public, max-age=31536000, immutable"
        return response

app.add_middleware(ImmutableAssetsCacheMiddleware)

# --- EXISTING: API routes remain unchanged ---
# app.include_router(api_router, prefix="/api")
# These are registered BEFORE the static mount, so they take priority.

# --- ADD: Health check (registered before any catch-all) ---
@app.get("/healthz")
async def healthz():
    return JSONResponse({"status": "ok"})

# --- ADD: Static assets mount ---
if DIST_ASSETS.exists():
    app.mount(
        "/assets",
        StaticFiles(directory=str(DIST_ASSETS)),
        name="static-assets"
    )

# --- ADD: SPA fallback via 404 exception handler ---
@app.exception_handler(StarletteHTTPException)
async def spa_or_api_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: StarletteHTTPException):
    if exc.status_code == 404:
        accept = request.headers.get("accept", "")
        if "text/html" in accept:
            return FileResponse(
                str(DIST_INDEX),
                headers={"Cache-Control": "no-cache"},
                media_type="text/html",
            )
    return JSONResponse(
        {"detail": exc.detail or "Not found"},
        status_code=exc.status_code,
    )
```

**Key design decisions**:
- `/healthz` is a dedicated endpoint returning JSON тАФ never masked by SPA fallback
- 404 handler uses `Accept` header content negotiation: browsers get `index.html`, API consumers get JSON
- `/assets/*` gets immutable cache headers via middleware since `StaticFiles` doesn't set them
- `index.html` always gets `Cache-Control: no-cache` (browser revalidates on every navigation)
- `validate_frontend_build()` is wired into FastAPI's `lifespan` context manager тАФ crashes on boot if `dist/` is missing
- Any new backend routes registered via `app.include_router()` or `@app.get()` take priority over the 404 handler тАФ no swallowing risk

### 2.2 Migration Strategy

**Phase 1 тАФ Parallel deploy**: Serve React app at `/v2/` path prefix. Vite builds with `base: '/v2/'`. Legacy Alpine app continues at `/`. Backend adds:
```python
if DIST_ASSETS.exists():
    app.mount("/v2/assets", StaticFiles(directory=str(DIST_ASSETS)), name="v2-assets")

@app.get("/v2")
@app.get("/v2/{path:path}")
async def spa_v2_fallback(path: str = ""):
    return FileResponse(str(DIST_INDEX), headers={"Cache-Control": "no-cache"})
```

**API contract during Phase 1**: All API endpoints return relative paths only (no absolute URLs). The React app at `/v2/` calls the same `/api/*` endpoints тАФ no base-path-aware rewriting needed because API routes are path-absolute (`/api/search`), not relative.

**Phase 2 тАФ Parity confirmed**: Change Vite `base` back to `'/'`. Swap `/` to serve React via the 404 exception handler. Move legacy Alpine files to `src/frontend/web/_legacy/`. Legacy remains accessible at `/_legacy/` for 2 weeks via a simple `StaticFiles` mount.

**Phase 3 тАФ Cleanup**: Remove `_legacy/` directory, remove `/v2` routes, remove legacy mount.

### 2.3 Rollback Plan

If React deploy fails after Phase 2 cutover:
1. Revert `web_server.py` to serve `_legacy/` at `/` (single config change)
2. `_legacy/` files are still present for 2-week window тАФ no rebuild required
3. Rollback is a server restart, not a deploy

### 2.4 Smoke Tests

```bash

curl -sf localhost:8000/healthz | grep '"status"'
# API routes still return JSON
curl -sf -H "Accept: application/json" localhost:8000/api/top-searches | head -c 1 | grep '['
# SPA serves HTML for browser requests
curl -sf -H "Accept: text/html" localhost:8000/ | grep '<div id="root">'
# Unknown paths serve SPA for browsers

# Unknown paths return JSON 404 for API consumers
curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -H "Accept: application/json" localhost:8000/nonexistent | grep 404
```

 ### 1.6 Navigation Flow Specification
 
 **Spatial model**: Stack-based navigation. Screens exist on a conceptual left-to-right axis ordered: Dashboard(0) тЖТ Search(1) тЖТ Document(2).
 
+**Direction detection**: `useNavigationDirection` tracks a monotonically incrementing position counter stored in `history.state.navPos`. Every `pushState` increments the counter. When `popstate` fires, the new position is compared to the last known position тАФ lower means back, higher means forward. This is immune to the non-linear history traversal bug (DashboardтЖТSearchтЖТDocumentтЖТSearch shows correct "back" animation because the position decremented).
+
+```javascript
+// shared/hooks/useNavigationDirection.js
+import { useRef, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react';
+
+let navPos = history.state?.navPos ?? 0;
+
+export function useNavigationDirection() {
+  const directionRef = useRef('forward');
+  const lastPosRef = useRef(navPos);
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    function onPopState(e) {
+      const newPos = e.state?.navPos ?? 0;
+      directionRef.current = newPos < lastPosRef.current ? 'back' : 'forward';
+      lastPosRef.current = newPos;
+    }
+    window.addEventListener('popstate', onPopState);
+    return () => window.removeEventListener('popstate', onPopState);
+  }, []);
+
+  const navigate = useCallback((url) => {
+    navPos += 1;
+    history.pushState({ navPos }, '', url);
+    lastPosRef.current = navPos;
+    directionRef.current = 'forward';
+  }, []);
+
+  return { directionRef, navigate };
+}
+```
+
 **Transition rules**:
-- Navigating to a higher-index screen: current screen slides left + fades, new screen slides in from right + fades in
-- Navigating to a lower-index screen (back): current screen slides right + fades, new screen slides in from left + fades in
-- Same-index navigation (e.g., Document тЖТ Document): crossfade only, no slide
-- ActionSheet: slides up from bottom (not a route transition)

 - `/healthz` is a dedicated endpoint returning JSON тАФ never masked by SPA fallback
-- 404 handler uses `Accept` header content negotiation: browsers get `index.html`, API consumers get JSON

 - `validate_frontend_build()` is wired into FastAPI's `lifespan` context manager тАФ crashes on boot if `dist/` is missing

objections.description: The artifact is truncated mid-sentence in the contrast compliance matrix. The entire section cuts off at '--text-link #8B7' тАФ meaning the contrast verification for link colors, error/success/warning semantic colors on their muted backgrounds, and the entire light theme contrast matrix are missing. Without verified contrast ratios for ALL token pairings in BOTH themes, this spec cannot claim WCAG 2.1 AA compliance. This is a shipping blocker: you're telling engineers 'these tokens are accessible' but haven't actually proven it for ~40% of the color combinations.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the contrast matrix for both dark and light themes covering: text-link on all backgrounds, text-primary/secondary on surface-selected/surface-critical/accent-muted, semantic colors (success/error/warning) on their muted backgrounds, and inverse text on accent. Also add the PROHIBITED usage rules for tertiary text on elevated surfaces (which was started but not completed).

objections.location: Section 1.5 - Component Architecture Map / API layer
objections.description: The api.js file is listed in the directory structure but never specified. There's no API client implementation, no error handling strategy for network failures, no retry policy, no request cancellation on unmount, and no typing of API response shapes. The useApiRequest hook is referenced in the state ownership table but never defined. Engineers will each invent their own fetch wrapper, leading to inconsistent error handling across features тАФ exactly the kind of 'wrong abstraction through duplication' that kills you at scale.
objections.suggested_fix: Define api.js with: (1) a base fetch wrapper that handles JSON parsing, non-2xx тЖТ thrown errors with status codes, and AbortController integration, (2) the useApiRequest hook implementation with loading/error/data states and automatic abort on unmount, (3) explicit error type definitions (NetworkError vs ApiError with status), (4) debounce strategy for autocomplete/suggest endpoints specifically. Show the hook contract so feature developers know exactly what they get.

objections.description: Search query lives as local state in SearchScreen, but there's no specification for URL synchronization. If a user searches for 'contract renewal', the URL should be /search?q=contract+renewal so the result is shareable and back-button works correctly. The current spec routes to /search with no query parameter handling. This is a UX regression from even the simplest search implementation and will require a painful retrofit if not designed in now.
objections.suggested_fix: Add query parameter syncing: SearchScreen reads initial query from URL search params via wouter's useSearch or window.location.search, and updates the URL (replaceState, not pushState for typing; pushState on submit) as the user types/submits. Define the exact URL shape: /search?q=encoded_query. This also means the SPA fallback in web_server.py correctly passes query strings through тАФ verify that the FileResponse approach preserves query params (it does, since the browser handles them client-side, but state it explicitly).

objections.location: Section 2.1 - SPA fallback via 404 exception handler
objections.description: Overriding the global 404 exception handler is dangerous. Any future developer adding a new API route that accidentally 404s (e.g., a typo in a path parameter handler) will get index.html served to API consumers if they happen to send Accept: text/html (which Postman and some HTTP clients do by default). The Accept header content negotiation is a reasonable heuristic but not bulletproof. More critically, this overrides ALL StarletteHTTPException handling тАФ a 403 or 500 from an API endpoint would lose its original status code if someone throws HTTPException(403) and the handler only checks for 404.

The motion system is described conceptually (slide left/right, crossfade, ActionSheet slides up) but the actual CSS animations are not defined anywhere in this spec. Section references 'animations.css' in the file tree but provides zero implementation. The convergence criteria explicitly require motion behavior approval. What are the exact durations? What easing curves (given that ease/linear are banned)? What are the CSS class names and keyframes? Without this, every agent is approving a promise, not a spec.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a Motion System section with: (1) the specific cubic-bezier curves used (e.g., cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1) for Flighty-style entries), (2) duration tokens as CSS custom properties (--duration-fast: 150ms, --duration-normal: 250ms, --duration-slow: 400ms), (3) the actual @keyframes for slide-in-right, slide-out-left, slide-up (ActionSheet), fade-in, fade-out, (4) prefers-reduced-motion handling that collapses all animations to instant/opacity-only, (5) the CSS class application logic in AnimatedRoute.

objections.description: Typography tokens are completely missing. For a spec that claims Nubank+Flighty premium aesthetic, there are zero font-family, font-size, line-height, letter-spacing, or font-weight tokens. Tailwind utility classes alone don't enforce consistency тАФ two engineers will pick text-sm vs text-xs for secondary labels and you'll get visual inconsistency across screens. Spacing scale is also absent.
objections.suggested_fix: Add typography tokens: --font-sans (system stack or specific typeface), --font-mono. Define a type scale with semantic names: --text-xs through --text-3xl with corresponding line-heights and letter-spacing. Define font-weight tokens: --weight-normal (400), --weight-medium (500), --weight-semibold (600), --weight-bold (700). Define spacing tokens if deviating from Tailwind defaults, or explicitly state 'spacing uses Tailwind's default 4px scale with no custom tokens'.

objections.description: Skeleton is listed as the Suspense fallback instead of spinners (good), but there's no specification of what the skeleton looks like per screen. A generic pulsing rectangle is barely better than a spinner. Each lazy-loaded screen needs a specific skeleton layout that matches its content structure to prevent CLS. The Skeleton component's variant prop ('text', 'card', 'circle', 'block') is too generic тАФ it needs screen-specific skeleton compositions.
objections.suggested_fix: Define skeleton compositions per screen: DashboardSkeleton (stat bars + card grid), SearchSkeleton (input + result list), DocumentSkeleton (header + timeline entries). These are the actual Suspense fallback components. The base Skeleton primitive is fine for composing them, but the compositions must be specified to prevent CLS тАФ each needs explicit height/width matching the real content's dimensions.

suggestions.description: BaseHTTPMiddleware has known performance issues in Starlette (it wraps every request in a thread). Since this middleware only needs to add a header to /assets/ paths, use a pure ASGI middleware or a simpler approach: configure StaticFiles with a custom headers parameter, or use Starlette's middleware stack with a lightweight implementation. At low traffic this doesn't matter, but it's worth noting for 10x scale.

The FOUC prevention script in index.html is mentioned in Section 3.8 but Section 3.8 doesn't exist in this artifact. Include the actual inline script: it should read localStorage, set data-theme on documentElement synchronously before React hydrates. Without seeing it, we can't verify it handles the edge case where localStorage is unavailable (Safari private browsing throws on setItem, not getItem, but still worth a try/catch).

The Phase 1 parallel deploy mounts /v2 as a catch-all route. This means /v2/api/search won't work тАФ the catch-all will serve index.html before the API router sees it. Either document that the React app at /v2 calls /api/* (not /v2/api/*), or ensure the route priority is explicit. The current spec implies this works correctly but doesn't state it clearly enough for an engineer implementing it.

Consider adding focus-trap or equivalent for the ActionSheet. Without focus trapping, keyboard users can Tab behind the open ActionSheet overlay into the DocumentScreen content тАФ an accessibility violation. Either specify a lightweight focus-trap utility (<1KB) or define the manual implementation using DOM focus management in the ActionSheet spec.

Relying on the Accept header for content negotiation is fragileтАФfetch requests typically send `Accept: */*` or include `text/html` with lower weight, causing API errors to return HTML instead of JSON. This breaks the API contract and prevents proper error handling in the frontend.
objections.suggested_fix: Check `request.url.path.startswith('/api')` before falling back to SPA HTML. API routes must return JSON 404s unconditionally, while non-API 404s serve index.html.

objections.description: No focus management strategy is defined for route transitions. The spec mentions aria-live announcements but fails to specify where focus moves after navigation (e.g., to H1, main landmark, or skip link). This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and 2.4.7 (Focus Visible), leaving keyboard users stranded at the previous route's location or at the bottom of the DOM.
objections.suggested_fix: Specify that AnimatedRoute must programmatically focus either the page H1 (with `tabIndex=-1`) or a 'route container' region with `role='main'` on every route change, ensuring focus is visible and logical.

objections.location: Section 3.1 Color Tokens / Contrast Compliance Matrix
objections.description: The contrast matrix explicitly marks `--text-tertiary` (#8E8E93) on `--bg-elevated` (#242428) as prohibited (тЭМ) with a failing ratio (~4.35:1), yet the token system allows this combination. This creates an accessibility trap where developers can legally combine tokens that produce unreadable text in dark mode.
objections.suggested_fix: Restrict the semantic token pairing system so `text-tertiary` is forbidden on `bg-elevated` surfaces, or adjust `--bg-elevated` to #2C2C30 or darker to achieve 4.5:1 minimum contrast.

objections.description: The navigation direction detection logic (comparing screen indices 0/1/2) fails for non-linear history traversal. When a user navigates Dashboard(0) тЖТ Search(1) тЖТ Document(2) тЖТ Search(1), the index decreases, triggering a 'back' slide animation even though the user is navigating forward to a previously visited page. This creates cognitive dissonance between the browser history and visual feedback.
objections.suggested_fix: Use the History API's `popstate` event to detect actual back/forward navigation direction, or maintain a history stack in sessionStorage to compare timestamps rather than static route indices.

objections.description: Making ActionSheet state-only (not URL-routable) violates the principle of least surprise for the browser back button. Users expect pressing back to dismiss an overlay/modal, not to navigate away from the Document entirely. This also prevents sharing direct links to specific action states (e.g., 'Edit document').
objections.suggested_fix: Implement ActionSheet as a URL query parameter (e.g., `/document/abc123?action=edit`) with `history.pushState` on open and `history.back()` on close, or handle `popstate` events to close the sheet when the user presses back.

objections.description: The artifact is truncated mid-token (`--color-text-link: #8B7`), making it impossible to verify WCAG compliance for light mode link colors or complete the design token review. This is a blocking issue for UX architecture sign-off.
objections.suggested_fix: Provide the complete tokens.css file including all light theme values and contrast calculations.

suggestions.description: Add a 'Skip to main content' link as the first focusable element in AppShell, visible only on focus, to bypass repetitive navigation for keyboard users.
Specify `prefers-reduced-motion` handling: when the user has reduced motion enabled, disable slide animations and use instant crossfades or no animation at all.
Clarify the debouncing strategy for SearchInput. Specify the delay (e.g., 150ms), cancellation behavior on unmount, and loading state transition timing to prevent race conditions.

The Timeline component lacks UX specification. Define: (1) Is it vertical or horizontal? (2) Are entries expandable? (3) How does keyboard navigation work between timeline nodes? (4) What is the maximum height before scrolling occurs?

objections.description: Contrast compliance matrix is truncated mid-row (--text-link #8B7... incomplete). Light theme has NO contrast verification at all - only dark theme tested. This violates the accessibility compliance convergence criterion.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the dark theme matrix and add full light theme contrast verification table. All text/background combinations must meet WCAG AA minimum (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text).

objections.description: Typography token system is completely missing. No font stack, no type scale (h1-h6, body, caption), no line-height tokens, no font-weight tokens. Cannot implement visual hierarchy without these.
objections.suggested_fix: Add complete typography token section with CSS custom properties: --font-family-sans, --font-family-mono, --text-xs through --text-2xl, --leading-tight through --leading-loose, --font-normal/medium/semibold/bold.

objections.description: Spacing system is completely absent. No spacing tokens, no layout grid, no container max-widths. Components cannot maintain consistent rhythm across breakpoints without this.
objections.suggested_fix: Add spacing token scale (--space-1 through --space-16 or --space-xs through --space-2xl) using 4px base unit. Add container widths for 375/768/1440 breakpoints.

objections.description: Motion token system referenced as deliverable but not defined. Spec bans ease/linear but provides NO alternative timing functions. No duration tokens, no easing curves, no reduced-motion handling.
objections.suggested_fix: Add motion tokens: --duration-fast (150ms), --duration-normal (250ms), --duration-slow (400ms), --ease-out-expo, --ease-in-out-quad. Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion) override section.

objections.description: --text-tertiary on bg-elevated marked PROHIBITED but no alternative provided. Designers will use it anyway without guidance.
objections.suggested_fix: Specify approved alternative: use --text-secondary on bg-elevated, or define --text-tertiary-elevated with verified contrast ratio.

objections.description: Section 3.8 (FOUC prevention inline script) referenced but not included. Section 6 (Skeleton variants) referenced but not detailed. Critical for dark-mode-first requirement.
objections.suggested_fix: Add complete Section 3.8 with inline script that reads localStorage/system preference before paint. Add Section 6 with Skeleton component CSS animation specs.

objections.description: StatusBadge uses color-only status indicators (success/warning/error/neutral). WCAG 1.4.1 requires non-color means of conveying information.
objections.suggested_fix: Add icon variants to StatusBadge (checkmark, exclamation, x, circle) alongside color. Ensure screen readers can distinguish status via aria-label.

objections.description: No elevation/shadow tokens despite 'elevated' surfaces referenced throughout component tree. Visual hierarchy will be inconsistent.
objections.suggested_fix: Add shadow token scale (--shadow-sm through --shadow-xl) with matching light/dark theme values. Document which components use which elevation level.

suggestions.description: Add border-radius token system (--radius-sm: 4px, --radius-md: 8px, --radius-lg: 12px, --radius-xl: 16px, --radius-full: 9999px) for consistent component styling.
Add z-index token scale (--z-dropdown, --z-sticky, --z-modal, --z-toast) to prevent stacking context conflicts between Toast, ActionSheet, and other overlays.
Document theme persistence strategy explicitly - localStorage key name, system preference detection order, and rehydration timing to prevent theme flash.

objections.description: Artifact is truncated mid-cell in the contrast matrix: `| --text-link #8B7`. The contrast compliance verification for link colors, success/error/warning colors, and light theme matrix are all missing. Cannot verify WCAG compliance without complete data.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the contrast compliance matrix for both themes, all semantic colors, and all background combinations.

objections.description: Artifact ends mid-section. Convergence criteria require approval of: motion behavior, accessibility compliance, component structure. Sections 3.2-3.8 (typography, spacing, elevation, radius, motion, breakpoints, FOUC prevention), Section 4 (core layout shell), Section 5 (key screens), Section 6 (skeleton/motion system) are all missing. This is Round 5 тАФ the spec should be complete.
objections.suggested_fix: Provide complete spec including: typography tokens, spacing scale, elevation system, border-radius tokens, motion/animation system with explicit timing functions (no ease/linear per requirements), responsive breakpoint implementations, and all 3 required screens (Dashboard, Document/Timeline, ActionSheet).

objections.description: Bundle size checker only validates JS files. CSS is not measured but contributes to total transfer size. A 160KB JS budget is meaningless if CSS adds another 50KB+. Also, `gzipSync({ level: 9 })` uses maximum compression which CDN/browsers typically don't use (usually level 6), making budgets optimistically small.
objections.suggested_fix: Add CSS file checking to the bundle script. Use level 6 compression to match real-world CDN behavior. Add CSS budget (suggest тЙд20KB gzipped for initial load).

objections.description: Code-splitting lazy imports show incorrect relative paths: `React.lazy(() => import('./features/search/SearchScreen'))` from `app/routes.js` would resolve to `app/features/search/SearchScreen` but the actual path is `src/features/search/SearchScreen`. Need correct relative path from app/routes.js.
objections.suggested_fix: Lazy imports should use `React.lazy(() => import('../features/search/SearchScreen'))` from routes.js location.

objections.description: Manual chunking only separates vendor. For a 3-screen app with lazy loading, feature chunks should be defined to prevent a single lazy chunk exceeding 40KB. Search, Dashboard, and Document screens should be separate chunks.
objections.suggested_fix: Add explicit chunk strategy: `manualChunks: { vendor: ['react', 'react-dom'], search: () => import('./features/search/DashboardScreen'), dashboard: () => import('./features/dashboard/DashboardScreen'), document: () => import('./features/document/DocumentScreen') }` or rely on dynamic imports with named output.

objections.description: Middleware sets immutable cache on all `/assets/*` paths but doesn't verify files actually have content hashes. If Vite config changes or build fails partway, unhashed files could be cached incorrectly.
objections.suggested_fix: Add validation in check-bundle-size.js that all files in assets/ contain content hashes (pattern: `[name]-[hash][ext]`). Fail build if any unhashed files exist.

suggestions.description: Consider pre-bundling react and react-dom into a single vendor chunk during build rather than treating them separately. Vite does this automatically but the budget table shows them as separate line items.
The navigation direction detection logic (`useNavigationDirection`) is mentioned but not implemented. This needs actual code to track history position changes.
The FOUC prevention inline script is referenced but not shown. This is critical for dark-mode-first approach. Include the actual script block.

unresolved_objections.description: The artifact is truncated mid-sentence in the contrast compliance matrix. The entire section cuts off at '--text-link #8B7' тАФ meaning the contrast verification for link colors, error/success/warning semantic colors on their muted backgrounds, and the entire light theme contrast matrix are missing. Without verified contrast ratios for ALL token pairings in BOTH themes, this spec cannot claim WCAG 2.1 AA compliance. This is a shipping blocker: you're telling engineers 'these tokens are accessible' but haven't actually proven it for ~40% of the color combinations.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Complete the contrast matrix for both dark and light themes covering: text-link on all backgrounds, text-primary/secondary on surface-selected/surface-critical/accent-muted, semantic colors (success/error/warning) on their muted backgrounds, and inverse text on accent. Also add the PROHIBITED usage rules for tertiary text on elevated surfaces (which was started but not completed).

unresolved_objections.location: Section 1.5 - Component Architecture Map / API layer
unresolved_objections.description: The api.js file is listed in the directory structure but never specified. There's no API client implementation, no error handling strategy for network failures, no retry policy, no request cancellation on unmount, and no typing of API response shapes. The useApiRequest hook is referenced in the state ownership table but never defined. Engineers will each invent their own fetch wrapper, leading to inconsistent error handling across features тАФ exactly the kind of 'wrong abstraction through duplication' that kills you at scale.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Define api.js with: (1) a base fetch wrapper that handles JSON parsing, non-2xx тЖТ thrown errors with status codes, and AbortController integration, (2) the useApiRequest hook implementation with loading/error/data states and automatic abort on unmount, (3) explicit error type definitions (NetworkError vs ApiError with status), (4) debounce strategy for autocomplete/suggest endpoints specifically. Show the hook contract so feature developers know exactly what they get.

unresolved_objections.description: Search query lives as local state in SearchScreen, but there's no specification for URL synchronization. If a user searches for 'contract renewal', the URL should be /search?q=contract+renewal so the result is shareable and back-button works correctly. The current spec routes to /search with no query parameter handling. This is a UX regression from even the simplest search implementation and will require a painful retrofit if not designed in now.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Add query parameter syncing: SearchScreen reads initial query from URL search params via wouter's useSearch or window.location.search, and updates the URL (replaceState, not pushState for typing; pushState on submit) as the user types/submits. Define the exact URL shape: /search?q=encoded_query. This also means the SPA fallback in web_server.py correctly passes query strings through тАФ verify that the FileResponse approach preserves query params (it does, since the browser handles them client-side, but state it explicitly).

unresolved_objections.location: Section 2.1 - SPA fallback via 404 exception handler
unresolved_objections.description: Overriding the global 404 exception handler is dangerous. Any future developer adding a new API route that accidentally 404s (e.g., a typo in a path parameter handler) will get index.html served to API consumers if they happen to send Accept: text/html (which Postman and some HTTP clients do by default). The Accept header content negotiation is a reasonable heuristic but not bulletproof. More critically, this overrides ALL StarletteHTTPException handling тАФ a 403 or 500 from an API endpoint would lose its original status code if someone throws HTTPException(403) and the handler only checks for 404.

The motion system is described conceptually (slide left/right, crossfade, ActionSheet slides up) but the actual CSS animations are not defined anywhere in this spec. Section references 'animations.css' in the file tree but provides zero implementation. The convergence criteria explicitly require motion behavior approval. What are the exact durations? What easing curves (given that ease/linear are banned)? What are the CSS class names and keyframes? Without this, every agent is approving a promise, not a spec.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Add a Motion System section with: (1) the specific cubic-bezier curves used (e.g., cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1) for Flighty-style entries), (2) duration tokens as CSS custom properties (--duration-fast: 150ms, --duration-normal: 250ms, --duration-slow: 400ms), (3) the actual @keyframes for slide-in-right, slide-out-left, slide-up (ActionSheet), fade-in, fade-out, (4) prefers-reduced-motion handling that collapses all animations to instant/opacity-only, (5) the CSS class application logic in AnimatedRoute.

unresolved_objections.description: Typography tokens are completely missing. For a spec that claims Nubank+Flighty premium aesthetic, there are zero font-family, font-size, line-height, letter-spacing, or font-weight tokens. Tailwind utility classes alone don't enforce consistency тАФ two engineers will pick text-sm vs text-xs for secondary labels and you'll get visual inconsistency across screens. Spacing scale is also absent.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Add typography tokens: --font-sans (system stack or specific typeface), --font-mono. Define a type scale with semantic names: --text-xs through --text-3xl with corresponding line-heights and letter-spacing. Define font-weight tokens: --weight-normal (400), --weight-medium (500), --weight-semibold (600), --weight-bold (700). Define spacing tokens if deviating from Tailwind defaults, or explicitly state 'spacing uses Tailwind's default 4px scale with no custom tokens'.

unresolved_objections.description: Skeleton is listed as the Suspense fallback instead of spinners (good), but there's no specification of what the skeleton looks like per screen. A generic pulsing rectangle is barely better than a spinner. Each lazy-loaded screen needs a specific skeleton layout that matches its content structure to prevent CLS. The Skeleton component's variant prop ('text', 'card', 'circle', 'block') is too generic тАФ it needs screen-specific skeleton compositions.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Define skeleton compositions per screen: DashboardSkeleton (stat bars + card grid), SearchSkeleton (input + result list), DocumentSkeleton (header + timeline entries). These are the actual Suspense fallback components. The base Skeleton primitive is fine for composing them, but the compositions must be specified to prevent CLS тАФ each needs explicit height/width matching the real content's dimensions.

unresolved_objections.description: The SPA fallback exception handler catches all 404 errors including legitimate API 404s (e.g., `/api/document/{invalid_id}`). Relying on the Accept header for content negotiation is fragileтАФfetch requests typically send `Accept: */*` or include `text/html` with lower weight, causing API errors to return HTML instead of JSON. This breaks the API contract and prevents proper error handling in the frontend.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Check `request.url.path.startswith('/api')` before falling back to SPA HTML. API routes must return JSON 404s unconditionally, while non-API 404s serve index.html.

unresolved_objections.description: No focus management strategy is defined for route transitions. The spec mentions aria-live announcements but fails to specify where focus moves after navigation (e.g., to H1, main landmark, or skip link). This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and 2.4.7 (Focus Visible), leaving keyboard users stranded at the previous route's location or at the bottom of the DOM.
unresolved_objections.suggested_fix: Specify that AnimatedRoute must programmatically focus either the page H1 (with `tabIndex=-1`) or a 'route container' region with `role='main'` on every route change, ensuring focus is visible and logical.
